authors_text: Lingzhi Zhang,Yuqian Zhou,Connelly Barnes,Sohrab Amirghodsi,Zhe Lin,Eli Shechtman,Jianbo Shi
title: Perceptual Artifacts Localization for Inpainting
title_zh: 修复的感知伪影定位
abstract: Image inpainting is an essential task for multiple practical applications like object removal and image editing. Deep GAN-based models greatly improve the inpainting performance in structures and textures within the hole, but might also generate unexpected artifacts like broken structures or color blobs. Users perceive these artifacts to judge the effectiveness of inpainting models, and retouch these imperfect areas to inpaint again in a typical retouching workflow. Inspired by this workflow, we propose a new learning task of automatic segmentation of inpainting perceptual artifacts, and apply the model for inpainting model evaluation and iterative refinement. Specifically, we first construct a new inpainting artifacts dataset by manually annotating perceptual artifacts in the results of state-of-the-art inpainting models. Then we train advanced segmentation networks on this dataset to reliably localize inpainting artifacts within inpainted images. Second, we propose a new interpretable evaluation metric called Perceptual Artifact Ratio (PAR), which is the ratio of objectionable inpainted regions to the entire inpainted area. PAR demonstrates a strong correlation with real user preference. Finally, we further apply the generated masks for iterative image inpainting by combining our approach with multiple recent inpainting methods. Extensive experiments demonstrate the consistent decrease of artifact regions and inpainting quality improvement across the different methods.
summary: Image inpainting is an essential task for multiple practical applications like object removal and image editing. Deep GAN-based models greatly improve the inpainting performance in structures and textures within the hole, but might also generate unexpected artifacts like broken structures or color blobs. Users perceive these artifacts to judge the effectiveness of inpainting models, and retouch these imperfect areas to inpaint again in a typical retouching workflow. Inspired by this workflow, we propose a new learning task of automatic segmentation of inpainting perceptual artifacts, and apply the model for inpainting model evaluation and iterative refinement. Specifically, we first construct a new inpainting artifacts dataset by manually annotating perceptual artifacts in the results of state-of-the-art inpainting models. Then we train advanced segmentation networks on this dataset to reliably localize inpainting artifacts within inpainted images. Second, we propose a new interpretable evaluation metric called Perceptual Artifact Ratio (PAR), which is the ratio of objectionable inpainted regions to the entire inpainted area. PAR demonstrates a strong correlation with real user preference. Finally, we further apply the generated masks for iterative image inpainting by combining our approach with multiple recent inpainting methods. Extensive experiments demonstrate the consistent decrease of artifact regions and inpainting quality improvement across the different methods.
abstract_zh: 图像修复是对象删除和图像编辑等多种实际应用的一项基本任务。基于深度 GAN 的模型极大地提高了孔内结构和纹理的修复性能，但也可能会产生意外的伪影，例如损坏的结构或颜色斑点。用户感知这些伪影来判断修复模型的有效性，并在典型的修饰工作流程中修饰这些不完美的区域以再次修复。受此工作流程的启发，我们提出了一种新的学习任务，即修复感知伪影的自动分割，并将该模型应用于修复模型评估和迭代细化。具体来说，我们首先通过在最先进的修复模型的结果中手动注释感知伪影来构建新的修复伪影数据集。然后，我们在此数据集上训练高级分割网络，以可靠地定位修复图像中的修复伪影。其次，我们提出了一种新的可解释的评估指标，称为感知伪影比（PAR），它是令人反感的修复区域与整个修复区域的比率。 PAR 与真实用户偏好有很强的相关性。最后，我们通过将我们的方法与多种最新的修复方法相结合，进一步将生成的掩模应用于迭代图像修复。大量的实验表明，不同方法中伪影区域的持续减少和修复质量的提高。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种自动定位图像修复中感知伪影的新方法，并用于模型评估和迭代优化。
AI.motivation: 现有深度修复模型虽能提升结构和纹理修复效果，但会产生结构性断裂或色块等伪影，需人工反复修正。本文旨在通过自动识别伪影实现模型评估与迭代优化。
AI.method: 构建人工标注的修复伪影数据集，训练分割网络定位伪影；提出感知伪影比率（PAR）作为可解释评估指标；结合多方法迭代修复生成更优结果。
AI.result: 实验表明该方法能有效减少伪影区域，提升不同修复方法的修复质量，且PAR与用户偏好高度相关。
AI.conclusion: 本文提出的伪影定位与迭代优化框架为修复模型评估和改进提供了自动化解决方案，显著提升修复效果。

PAR demonstrates a strong correlation with real user preference. Finally, we further apply the generated masks for iterative image inpainting by combining our approach with multiple recent inpainting methods. Extensive experiments demonstrate the consistent decrease of artifact regions and inpainting quality improvement across the different methods. 图像修复是对象删除和图像编辑等多种实际应用的一项基本任务。基于深度 GAN 的模型极大地提高了孔内结构和纹理的修复性能，但也可能会产生意外的伪影，例如损坏的结构或颜色斑点。用户感知这些伪影来判断修复模型的有效性，并在典型的修饰工作流程中修饰这些不完美的区域以再次修复。受此工作流程的启发，我们提出了一种新的学习任务，即修复感知伪影的自动分割，并将该模型应用于修复模型评估和迭代细化。具体来说，我们首先通过在最先进的修复模型的结果中手动注释感知伪影来构建新的修复伪影数据集。然后，我们在此数据集上训练高级分割网络，以可靠地定位修复图像中的修复伪影。其次，我们提出了一种新的可解释的评估指标，称为感知伪影比（PAR），它是令人反感的修复区域与整个修复区域的比率。 PAR 与真实用户偏好有很强的相关性。最后，我们通过将我们的方法与多种最新的修复方法相结合，进一步将生成的掩模应用于迭代图像修复。大量的实验表明，不同方法中伪影区域的持续减少和修复质量的提高。 本文提出一种自动定位图像修复中感知伪影的新方法，并用于模型评估和迭代优化。 Lingzhi Zhang,Yuqian Zhou,Connelly Barnes,Sohrab Amirghodsi,Zhe Lin,Eli Shechtman,Jianbo Shi cs.CV

authors_text: Patsorn Sangkloy,Wittawat Jitkrittum,Diyi Yang,James Hays
title: A Sketch Is Worth a Thousand Words: Image Retrieval with Text and Sketch
title_zh: 一草胜千言：文本与草图的图像检索
abstract: We address the problem of retrieving images with both a sketch and a text query. We present TASK-former (Text And SKetch transformer), an end-to-end trainable model for image retrieval using a text description and a sketch as input. We argue that both input modalities complement each other in a manner that cannot be achieved easily by either one alone. TASK-former follows the late-fusion dual-encoder approach, similar to CLIP, which allows efficient and scalable retrieval since the retrieval set can be indexed independently of the queries. We empirically demonstrate that using an input sketch (even a poorly drawn one) in addition to text considerably increases retrieval recall compared to traditional text-based image retrieval. To evaluate our approach, we collect 5,000 hand-drawn sketches for images in the test set of the COCO dataset. The collected sketches are available a https://janesjanes.github.io/tsbir/.
summary: We address the problem of retrieving images with both a sketch and a text query. We present TASK-former (Text And SKetch transformer), an end-to-end trainable model for image retrieval using a text description and a sketch as input. We argue that both input modalities complement each other in a manner that cannot be achieved easily by either one alone. TASK-former follows the late-fusion dual-encoder approach, similar to CLIP, which allows efficient and scalable retrieval since the retrieval set can be indexed independently of the queries. We empirically demonstrate that using an input sketch (even a poorly drawn one) in addition to text considerably increases retrieval recall compared to traditional text-based image retrieval. To evaluate our approach, we collect 5,000 hand-drawn sketches for images in the test set of the COCO dataset. The collected sketches are available a https://janesjanes.github.io/tsbir/.
abstract_zh: 我们解决了使用草图和文本查询检索图像的问题。我们提出了 TASK-former（文本和 SKetch 转换器），这是一种使用文本描述和草图作为输入进行图像检索的端到端可训练模型。我们认为，这两种输入方式是相辅相成的，而单独使用任何一种方式都无法轻松实现。 TASK-former 遵循后期融合双编码器方法，类似于 CLIP，它允许高效且可扩展的检索，因为检索集可以独立于查询进行索引。我们凭经验证明，与传统的基于文本的图像检索相比，除了文本之外，使用输入草图（即使是画得不好的草图）也能显着提高检索召回率。为了评估我们的方法，我们收集了 COCO 数据集测试集中的 5,000 个图像手绘草图。收集的草图可在 https://janesjanes.github.io/tsbir/ 上获取。

AI.tldr: 本文提出TASK-former模型，通过结合文本描述和草图输入实现更高效的图像检索。
AI.motivation: 传统文本图像检索存在语义歧义问题，而草图能补充视觉细节，两者结合可提升检索准确性。
AI.method: 采用晚期融合双编码器架构（类似CLIP），独立编码文本-草图模态后再进行融合，支持大规模高效检索。
AI.result: 在COCO数据集上收集5000个手绘草图验证，实验表明加入草图后检索召回率显著提升，且对粗糙草图具有鲁棒性。
AI.conclusion: 多模态融合（文本+草图）能有效增强图像检索性能，为弱监督检索提供新思路。

authors_text: Federico Rossi,Dylan Shell
title: Planning under periodic observations: bounds and bounding-based solutions
title_zh: 定期观察下的规划：边界和基于边界的解决方案
abstract: We study planning problems faced by robots operating in uncertain environments with incomplete knowledge of state, and actions that are noisy and/or imprecise. This paper identifies a new problem sub-class that models settings in which information is revealed only intermittently through some exogenous process that provides state information periodically. Several practical domains fit this model, including the specific scenario that motivates our research: autonomous navigation of a planetary exploration rover augmented by remote imaging. With an eye to efficient specialized solution methods, we examine the structure of instances of this sub-class. They lead to Markov Decision Processes with exponentially large action-spaces but for which, as those actions comprise sequences of more atomic elements, one may establish performance bounds by comparing policies under different information assumptions. This provides a way in which to construct performance bounds systematically. Such bounds are useful because, in conjunction with the insights they confer, they can be employed in bounding-based methods to obtain high-quality solutions efficiently; the empirical results we present demonstrate their effectiveness for the considered problems. The foregoing has also alluded to the distinctive role that time plays for these problems -- more specifically: time until information is revealed -- and we uncover and discuss several interesting subtleties in this regard.
summary: We study planning problems faced by robots operating in uncertain environments with incomplete knowledge of state, and actions that are noisy and/or imprecise. This paper identifies a new problem sub-class that models settings in which information is revealed only intermittently through some exogenous process that provides state information periodically. Several practical domains fit this model, including the specific scenario that motivates our research: autonomous navigation of a planetary exploration rover augmented by remote imaging. With an eye to efficient specialized solution methods, we examine the structure of instances of this sub-class. They lead to Markov Decision Processes with exponentially large action-spaces but for which, as those actions comprise sequences of more atomic elements, one may establish performance bounds by comparing policies under different information assumptions. This provides a way in which to construct performance bounds systematically. Such bounds are useful because, in conjunction with the insights they confer, they can be employed in bounding-based methods to obtain high-quality solutions efficiently; the empirical results we present demonstrate their effectiveness for the considered problems. The foregoing has also alluded to the distinctive role that time plays for these problems -- more specifically: time until information is revealed -- and we uncover and discuss several interesting subtleties in this regard.
abstract_zh: 我们研究在状态知识不完整的不确定环境中运行的机器人所面临的规划问题，以及嘈杂和/或不精确的动作。本文确定了一个新的问题子类，该子类对设置进行建模，其中信息仅通过某些定期提供状态信息的外生过程间歇性地显示。几个实际领域适合该模型，包括激发我们研究的具体场景：通过远程成像增强的行星探测车的自主导航。着眼于高效的专门解决方法，我们检查了该子类的实例的结构。它们导致马尔可夫决策过程具有指数级大的动作空间，但由于这些动作包含更多原子元素的序列，因此可以通过比较不同信息假设下的策略来建立性能界限。这提供了一种系统地构建性能界限的方法。这种界限很有用，因为结合它们所提供的见解，它们可以用于基于界限的方法，以有效地获得高质量的解决方案；我们提出的实证结果证明了它们对于所考虑的问题的有效性。前述内容还暗示了时间在这些问题中发挥的独特作用 - 更具体地说：信息披露之前的时间 - 我们发现并讨论了这方面的几个有趣的微妙之处。

AI.tldr: 本文研究了在信息仅通过外部过程周期性揭示的不确定环境中，机器人规划问题的新子类，并提出基于性能边界的求解方法。
AI.motivation: 现实场景中（如行星探测车远程成像导航），机器人需在状态信息不完整且动作存在噪声的情况下进行规划，传统方法难以高效处理此类周期性信息更新的问题。
AI.method: 通过分析问题结构，建立具有指数级动作空间的马尔可夫决策过程（MDP），并基于不同信息假设下的策略比较，系统构建性能边界以指导求解。
AI.result: 实验验证了基于性能边界的求解方法在目标问题中的有效性，能够高效生成高质量解决方案。
AI.conclusion: 时间因素（信息更新间隔）对问题求解具有关键影响，本文方法通过性能边界分析为周期性信息场景提供了有效解决框架。

authors_text: Nikolas Martelaro
title: Exploring the Future of Remote User Research
title_zh: 探索远程用户研究的未来
comment: 18 pages, 1 figure, 1 table, Presented at the Microsoft Research Symposium on the New Future of Work 2020, August 3-5, 2020
comments: 18 pages, 1 figure, 1 table, Presented at the Microsoft Research Symposium on the New Future of Work 2020, August 3-5, 2020
abstract: The rapid move to remote work due to COVID-19 social distancing policies has slowed or stopped most in-person qualitative user research activities. The limitation on in-person activities has pushed many user researchers to consider how they can continue their research remotely and presents an opportunity for user research teams to (re)design their research processes for remote work. In this position statement, I outline prior work in remote qualitative user research methods and discuss how this work can help us to (re)design new tools, methods, and frameworks that can enable remote work. I then propose a set of research questions that can further our knowledge and abilities to conduct effective remote user research. With creative development, we can create remote research methods that afford new ways of understanding user experience today and build more environmentally conscious and accessible research practices for a future where remote user research is much more common.
summary: The rapid move to remote work due to COVID-19 social distancing policies has slowed or stopped most in-person qualitative user research activities. The limitation on in-person activities has pushed many user researchers to consider how they can continue their research remotely and presents an opportunity for user research teams to (re)design their research processes for remote work. In this position statement, I outline prior work in remote qualitative user research methods and discuss how this work can help us to (re)design new tools, methods, and frameworks that can enable remote work. I then propose a set of research questions that can further our knowledge and abilities to conduct effective remote user research. With creative development, we can create remote research methods that afford new ways of understanding user experience today and build more environmentally conscious and accessible research practices for a future where remote user research is much more common.
abstract_zh: 由于 COVID-19 社交距离政策，人们迅速转向远程工作，这已经减缓或停止了大多数面对面的定性用户研究活动。面对面活动的限制促使许多用户研究人员考虑如何远程继续他们的研究，并为用户研究团队提供了（重新）设计远程工作研究流程的机会。在这份立场声明中，我概述了远程定性用户研究方法的先前工作，并讨论了这项工作如何帮助我们（重新）设计能够实现远程工作的新工具、方法和框架。然后，我提出了一系列研究问题，可以加深我们的知识和能力，以进行有效的远程用户研究。通过创造性的发展，我们可以创建远程研究方法，提供理解当今用户体验的新方法，并为远程用户研究更加普遍的未来建立更加环保和易于访问的研究实践。

AI.tldr: 本文探讨了后疫情时代远程用户研究方法的未来发展，提出重新设计研究流程以适应远程工作需求。
AI.motivation: 新冠疫情导致的社交隔离政策中断了传统面对面用户研究，迫使研究者探索远程研究方法的创新与优化。
AI.method: 通过回顾远程定性用户研究的现有文献，分析现有方法局限性，并提出新的研究问题与框架设计。
AI.result: 提出了推动远程用户研究有效性的关键问题，以及更环保、可及的未来研究实践方向。
AI.conclusion: 通过创造性开发，远程研究方法可实现更深入的用户体验洞察，并建立可持续的研究范式。

search_text: Exploring the Future of Remote User Research 探索远程用户研究的未来 The rapid move to remote work due to COVID-19 social distancing policies has slowed or stopped most in-person qualitative user research activities. The limitation on in-person activities has pushed many user researchers to consider how they can continue their research remotely and presents an opportunity for user research teams to (re)design their research processes for remote work. In this position statement, I outline prior work in remote qualitative user research methods and discuss how this work can help us to (re)design new tools, methods, and frameworks that can enable remote work. I then propose a set of research questions that can further our knowledge and abilities to conduct effective remote user research. With creative development, we can create remote research methods that afford new ways of understanding user experience today and build more environmentally conscious and accessible research practices for a future where remote user research is much more common. 由于 COVID-19 社交距离政策，人们迅速转向远程工作，这已经减缓或停止了大多数面对面的定性用户研究活动。面对面活动的限制促使许多用户研究人员考虑如何远程继续他们的研究，并为用户研究团队提供了（重新）设计远程工作研究流程的机会。在这份立场声明中，我概述了远程定性用户研究方法的先前工作，并讨论了这项工作如何帮助我们（重新）设计能够实现远程工作的新工具、方法和框架。然后，我提出了一系列研究问题，可以加深我们的知识和能力，以进行有效的远程用户研究。通过创造性的发展，我们可以创建远程研究方法，提供理解当今用户体验的新方法，并为远程用户研究更加普遍的未来建立更加环保和易于访问的研究实践。 本文探讨了后疫情时代远程用户研究方法的未来发展，提出重新设计研究流程以适应远程工作需求。 Nikolas Martelaro cs.HC

authors_text: Fransisca Susan,Negin Golrezaei,Ehsan Emamjomeh-Zadeh,David Kempe
title: Active Learning for Non-Parametric Choice Models
title_zh: 非参数选择模型的主动学习
abstract: We study the problem of actively learning a non-parametric choice model based on consumers' decisions. We present a negative result showing that such choice models may not be identifiable. To overcome the identifiability problem, we introduce a directed acyclic graph (DAG) representation of the choice model. This representation provably encodes all the information about the choice model which can be inferred from the available data, in the sense that it permits computing all choice probabilities. We establish that given exact choice probabilities for a collection of item sets, one can reconstruct the DAG. However, attempting to extend this methodology to estimate the DAG from noisy choice frequency data obtained during an active learning process leads to inaccuracies. To address this challenge, we present an inclusion-exclusion approach that effectively manages error propagation across DAG levels, leading to a more accurate estimate of the DAG. Utilizing this technique, our algorithm estimates the DAG representation of an underlying non-parametric choice model. The algorithm operates efficiently (in polynomial time) when the set of frequent rankings is drawn uniformly at random. It learns the distribution over the most popular items among frequent preference types by actively and repeatedly offering assortments of items and observing the chosen item. We demonstrate that our algorithm more effectively recovers a set of frequent preferences on both synthetic and publicly available datasets on consumers' preferences, compared to corresponding non-active learning estimation algorithms. These findings underscore the value of our algorithm and the broader applicability of active-learning approaches in modeling consumer behavior.
summary: We study the problem of actively learning a non-parametric choice model based on consumers' decisions. We present a negative result showing that such choice models may not be identifiable. To overcome the identifiability problem, we introduce a directed acyclic graph (DAG) representation of the choice model. This representation provably encodes all the information about the choice model which can be inferred from the available data, in the sense that it permits computing all choice probabilities. We establish that given exact choice probabilities for a collection of item sets, one can reconstruct the DAG. However, attempting to extend this methodology to estimate the DAG from noisy choice frequency data obtained during an active learning process leads to inaccuracies. To address this challenge, we present an inclusion-exclusion approach that effectively manages error propagation across DAG levels, leading to a more accurate estimate of the DAG. Utilizing this technique, our algorithm estimates the DAG representation of an underlying non-parametric choice model. The algorithm operates efficiently (in polynomial time) when the set of frequent rankings is drawn uniformly at random. It learns the distribution over the most popular items among frequent preference types by actively and repeatedly offering assortments of items and observing the chosen item. We demonstrate that our algorithm more effectively recovers a set of frequent preferences on both synthetic and publicly available datasets on consumers' preferences, compared to corresponding non-active learning estimation algorithms. These findings underscore the value of our algorithm and the broader applicability of active-learning approaches in modeling consumer behavior.
abstract_zh: 我们研究主动学习基于消费者决策的非参数选择模型的问题。我们提出了一个负面结果，表明这种选择模型可能无法识别。为了克服可识别性问题，我们引入了选择模型的有向无环图（DAG）表示。这种表示可证明地编码了有关选择模型的所有信息，这些信息可以从可用数据中推断出来，从某种意义上说，它允许计算所有选择概率。我们确定，给定一组项目集的精确选择概率，我们可以重建 DAG。然而，尝试扩展这种方法来根据主动学习过程中获得的噪声选择频率数据来估计 DAG 会导致不准确。为了应对这一挑战，我们提出了一种包含排除方法，可以有效管理跨 DAG 级别的错误传播，从而更准确地估计 DAG。利用这种技术，我们的算法可以估计底层非参数选择模型的 DAG 表示。当频繁均匀地随机抽取一组频繁排名时，该算法可以高效运行（在多项式时间内）。它通过主动、重复地提供各种项目并观察所选项目来学习频繁偏好类型中最受欢迎的项目的分布。我们证明，与相应的非主动学习估计算法相比，我们的算法可以更有效地恢复关于消费者偏好的合成和公开数据集上的一组频繁偏好。这些发现强调了我们的算法的价值以及主动学习方法在消费者行为建模中的更广泛适用性。

AI.tldr: 本文研究基于消费者决策主动学习非参数选择模型的问题，提出通过有向无环图（DAG）表示和包含-排除方法解决模型不可识别性及噪声数据挑战。
AI.motivation: 非参数选择模型可能存在不可识别性问题，传统方法在噪声数据下难以准确估计模型结构，需设计更鲁棒的主动学习算法。
AI.method: 引入DAG编码选择模型信息，提出包含-排除方法控制误差传播，并通过主动学习策略高效估计DAG结构。
AI.result: 算法在合成数据和真实消费者偏好数据集上均优于非主动学习方法，能更准确恢复高频偏好集合。
AI.conclusion: 主动学习方法有效提升非参数选择模型的估计精度，DAG表示与误差控制技术为消费者行为建模提供新思路。

authors_text: Mokanarangan Thayaparan,Marco Valentino,André Freitas
title: Going Beyond Approximation: Encoding Constraints for Explainable Multi-hop Inference via Differentiable Combinatorial Solvers
title_zh: 超越近似：通过可微组合求解器进行可解释多跳推理的编码约束
abstract: Integer Linear Programming (ILP) provides a viable mechanism to encode explicit and controllable assumptions about explainable multi-hop inference with natural language. However, an ILP formulation is non-differentiable and cannot be integrated into broader deep learning architectures. Recently, Thayaparan et al. (2021a) proposed a novel methodology to integrate ILP with Transformers to achieve end-to-end differentiability for complex multi-hop inference. While this hybrid framework has been demonstrated to deliver better answer and explanation selection than transformer-based and existing ILP solvers, the neuro-symbolic integration still relies on a convex relaxation of the ILP formulation, which can produce sub-optimal solutions. To improve these limitations, we propose Diff-Comb Explainer, a novel neuro-symbolic architecture based on Differentiable BlackBox Combinatorial solvers (DBCS) (Pogančić et al., 2019). Unlike existing differentiable solvers, the presented model does not require the transformation and relaxation of the explicit semantic constraints, allowing for direct and more efficient integration of ILP formulations. Diff-Comb Explainer demonstrates improved accuracy and explainability over non-differentiable solvers, Transformers and existing differentiable constraint-based multi-hop inference frameworks.
summary: Integer Linear Programming (ILP) provides a viable mechanism to encode explicit and controllable assumptions about explainable multi-hop inference with natural language. However, an ILP formulation is non-differentiable and cannot be integrated into broader deep learning architectures. Recently, Thayaparan et al. (2021a) proposed a novel methodology to integrate ILP with Transformers to achieve end-to-end differentiability for complex multi-hop inference. While this hybrid framework has been demonstrated to deliver better answer and explanation selection than transformer-based and existing ILP solvers, the neuro-symbolic integration still relies on a convex relaxation of the ILP formulation, which can produce sub-optimal solutions. To improve these limitations, we propose Diff-Comb Explainer, a novel neuro-symbolic architecture based on Differentiable BlackBox Combinatorial solvers (DBCS) (Pogančić et al., 2019). Unlike existing differentiable solvers, the presented model does not require the transformation and relaxation of the explicit semantic constraints, allowing for direct and more efficient integration of ILP formulations. Diff-Comb Explainer demonstrates improved accuracy and explainability over non-differentiable solvers, Transformers and existing differentiable constraint-based multi-hop inference frameworks.
abstract_zh: 整数线性规划（ILP）提供了一种可行的机制，可以用自然语言对可解释的多跳推理的显式且可控的假设进行编码。然而，ILP 公式是不可微分的，无法集成到更广泛的深度学习架构中。最近，塔亚帕兰等人。 (2021a) 提出了一种将 ILP 与 Transformer 集成的新颖方法，以实现复杂多跳推理的端到端可微性。虽然这种混合框架已被证明可以提供比基于变压器和现有 ILP 求解器更好的答案和解释选择，但神经符号集成仍然依赖于 ILP 公式的凸松弛，这可能会产生次优解决方案。为了改善这些限制，我们提出了 Diff-Comb Expander，这是一种基于可微分黑盒组合求解器 (DBCS) 的新型神经符号架构 (Pogančić et al., 2019)。与现有的可微分求解器不同，所提出的模型不需要显式语义约束的转换和松弛，从而允许直接且更有效地集成 ILP 公式。 Diff-Comb Expander 展示了比不可微分求解器、Transformer 和现有的基于可微分约束的多跳推理框架更高的准确性和可解释性。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于可微分组合求解器的神经符号架构Diff-Comb Explainer，用于提升可解释多跳推理的准确性与可解释性。
AI.motivation: 现有方法在将整数线性规划（ILP）与深度学习结合时依赖凸松弛技术，导致次优解且无法直接集成显式语义约束。
AI.method: 通过不同iable黑盒组合求解器（DBCS）直接集成ILP公式，无需约束转换与松弛，实现端到端可微分的神经符号系统。
AI.result: 在非微分求解器、Transformer及现有可微分框架上表现出更高的准确率与可解释性。
AI.conclusion: Diff-Comb Explainer有效解决了ILP与深度学习的集成难题，在保持约束显式性的同时提升了推理性能。

search_text: Going Beyond Approximation: Encoding Constraints for Explainable Multi-hop Inference via Differentiable Combinatorial Solvers 超越近似：通过可微组合求解器进行可解释多跳推理的编码约束 Integer Linear Programming (ILP) provides a viable mechanism to encode explicit and controllable assumptions about explainable multi-hop inference with natural language. However, an ILP formulation is non-differentiable and cannot be integrated into broader deep learning architectures. Recently, Thayaparan et al. (2021a) proposed a novel methodology to integrate ILP with Transformers to achieve end-to-end differentiability for complex multi-hop inference. While this hybrid framework has been demonstrated to deliver better answer and explanation selection than transformer-based and existing ILP solvers, the neuro-symbolic integration still relies on a convex relaxation of the ILP formulation, which can produce sub-optimal solutions. To improve these limitations, we propose Diff-Comb Explainer, a novel neuro-symbolic architecture based on Differentiable BlackBox Combinatorial solvers (DBCS) (Pogančić et al., 2019). Unlike existing differentiable solvers, the presented model does not require the transformation and relaxation of the explicit semantic constraints, allowing for direct and more efficient integration of ILP formulations. Diff-Comb Explainer demonstrates improved accuracy and explainability over non-differentiable solvers, Transformers and existing differentiable constraint-based multi-hop inference frameworks. 整数线性规划（ILP）提供了一种可行的机制，可以用自然语言对可解释的多跳推理的显式且可控的假设进行编码。然而，ILP 公式是不可微分的，无法集成到更广泛的深度学习架构中。最近，塔亚帕兰等人。 (2021a) 提出了一种将 ILP 与 Transformer 集成的新颖方法，以实现复杂多跳推理的端到端可微性。虽然这种混合框架已被证明可以提供比基于变压器和现有 ILP 求解器更好的答案和解释选择，但神经符号集成仍然依赖于 ILP 公式的凸松弛，这可能会产生次优解决方案。为了改善这些限制，我们提出了 Diff-Comb Expander，这是一种基于可微分黑盒组合求解器 (DBCS) 的新型神经符号架构 (Pogančić et al., 2019)。与现有的可微分求解器不同，所提出的模型不需要显式语义约束的转换和松弛，从而允许直接且更有效地集成 ILP 公式。 Diff-Comb Expander 展示了比不可微分求解器、Transformer 和现有的基于可微分约束的多跳推理框架更高的准确性和可解释性。 本文提出了一种基于可微分组合求解器的神经符号架构Diff-Comb Explainer，用于提升可解释多跳推理的准确性与可解释性。 Mokanarangan Thayaparan,Marco Valentino,André Freitas cs.AI,cs.CL

authors_text: Marawan Elbatel,Christina Bornberg,Manasi Kattel,Enrique Almar,Claudio Marrocco,Alessandro Bria
title: Seamless Iterative Semi-Supervised Correction of Imperfect Labels in Microscopy Images
title_zh: 显微图像中不完美标签的无缝迭代半监督校正
comment: To appear at MICCAI 2022 Workshop on Domain Adaptation and Representation Transfer (DART)
comments: To appear at MICCAI 2022 Workshop on Domain Adaptation and Representation Transfer (DART)
abstract: In-vitro tests are an alternative to animal testing for the toxicity of medical devices. Detecting cells as a first step, a cell expert evaluates the growth of cells according to cytotoxicity grade under the microscope. Thus, human fatigue plays a role in error making, making the use of deep learning appealing. Due to the high cost of training data annotation, an approach without manual annotation is needed. We propose Seamless Iterative Semi-Supervised correction of Imperfect labels (SISSI), a new method for training object detection models with noisy and missing annotations in a semi-supervised fashion. Our network learns from noisy labels generated with simple image processing algorithms, which are iteratively corrected during self-training. Due to the nature of missing bounding boxes in the pseudo labels, which would negatively affect the training, we propose to train on dynamically generated synthetic-like images using seamless cloning. Our method successfully provides an adaptive early learning correction technique for object detection. The combination of early learning correction that has been applied in classification and semantic segmentation before and synthetic-like image generation proves to be more effective than the usual semi-supervised approach by > 15% AP and > 20% AR across three different readers. Our code is available at https://github.com/marwankefah/SISSI.
summary: In-vitro tests are an alternative to animal testing for the toxicity of medical devices. Detecting cells as a first step, a cell expert evaluates the growth of cells according to cytotoxicity grade under the microscope. Thus, human fatigue plays a role in error making, making the use of deep learning appealing. Due to the high cost of training data annotation, an approach without manual annotation is needed. We propose Seamless Iterative Semi-Supervised correction of Imperfect labels (SISSI), a new method for training object detection models with noisy and missing annotations in a semi-supervised fashion. Our network learns from noisy labels generated with simple image processing algorithms, which are iteratively corrected during self-training. Due to the nature of missing bounding boxes in the pseudo labels, which would negatively affect the training, we propose to train on dynamically generated synthetic-like images using seamless cloning. Our method successfully provides an adaptive early learning correction technique for object detection. The combination of early learning correction that has been applied in classification and semantic segmentation before and synthetic-like image generation proves to be more effective than the usual semi-supervised approach by > 15% AP and > 20% AR across three different readers. Our code is available at https://github.com/marwankefah/SISSI.
abstract_zh: 体外测试是医疗器械毒性动物测试的替代方法。作为检测细胞的第一步，细胞专家根据显微镜下的细胞毒性等级评估细胞的生长。因此，人类疲劳在错误发生中发挥着重要作用，这使得深度学习的使用具有吸引力。由于训练数据标注的成本很高，因此需要一种无需手动标注的方法。我们提出了不完美标签的无缝迭代半监督校正（SISSI），这是一种以半监督方式训练带有噪声和缺失注释的对象检测模型的新方法。我们的网络从使用简单图像处理算法生成的噪声标签中学习，这些标签在自我训练过程中进行迭代校正。由于伪标签中缺少边界框的性质会对训练产生负面影响，因此我们建议使用无缝克隆对动态生成的类合成图像进行训练。我们的方法成功地为目标检测提供了一种自适应早期学习校正技术。事实证明，之前应用于分类和语义分割的早期学习校正与合成图像生成的结合比通常的半监督方法更有效，在三个不同的读者中，AP 超过 15%，AR 超过 20%。我们的代码可在 https://github.com/marwankefah/SISSI 获取。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种半监督方法SISSI，用于在显微镜图像中修正不完美标签，以提高细胞检测的准确性。
AI.motivation: 显微镜下细胞毒性评估依赖人工标注，但人工疲劳导致标签错误且标注成本高，需自动化解决方案。
AI.method: 通过迭代自训练修正噪声标签，并利用无缝克隆生成类似合成图像以应对伪标签中缺失的边界框问题。
AI.result: 在三种不同标注者数据集上，SISSI相较常规半监督方法提升AP超过15%、AR超过20%。
AI.conclusion: 结合早期学习修正与合成图像生成的SISSI方法，有效解决了显微图像中不完美标签的训练难题。

authors_text: Michele Cozzatti,Federico Simonetta,Stavros Ntalampiras
title: Variational Autoencoders for Anomaly Detection in Respiratory Sounds
title_zh: 用于呼吸音异常检测的变分自动编码器
abstract: This paper proposes a weakly-supervised machine learning-based approach aiming at a tool to alert patients about possible respiratory diseases. Various types of pathologies may affect the respiratory system, potentially leading to severe diseases and, in certain cases, death. In general, effective prevention practices are considered as major actors towards the improvement of the patient's health condition. The proposed method strives to realize an easily accessible tool for the automatic diagnosis of respiratory diseases. Specifically, the method leverages Variational Autoencoder architectures permitting the usage of training pipelines of limited complexity and relatively small-sized datasets. Importantly, it offers an accuracy of 57 %, which is in line with the existing strongly-supervised approaches.
summary: This paper proposes a weakly-supervised machine learning-based approach aiming at a tool to alert patients about possible respiratory diseases. Various types of pathologies may affect the respiratory system, potentially leading to severe diseases and, in certain cases, death. In general, effective prevention practices are considered as major actors towards the improvement of the patient's health condition. The proposed method strives to realize an easily accessible tool for the automatic diagnosis of respiratory diseases. Importantly, it offers an accuracy of 57 %, which is in line with the existing strongly-supervised approaches.
abstract_zh: 本文提出了一种基于弱监督机器学习的方法，旨在开发一种工具来提醒患者可能患有呼吸道疾病。各种类型的病理可能会影响呼吸系统，可能导致严重疾病，在某些情况下甚至导致死亡。一般来说，有效的预防措施被认为是改善患者健康状况的主要因素。该方法致力于实现一种易于使用的呼吸系统疾病自动诊断工具。具体来说，该方法利用变分自动编码器架构，允许使用复杂性有限的训练管道和相对较小的数据集。重要的是，它的准确率为 57%，这与现有的强监督方法一致。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于变分自编码器的弱监督方法，用于呼吸音异常检测，实现呼吸道疾病的自动诊断。
AI.motivation: 呼吸道疾病可能引发严重后果，现有强监督方法依赖大量标注数据且复杂度高，需开发更易用的小数据集适用工具。
AI.method: 采用变分自编码器架构，通过弱监督训练流程处理小规模数据集，利用重构误差进行异常检测。
AI.result: 在测试中达到57%的准确率，与强监督方法性能相当。
AI.conclusion: 该方法为呼吸道疾病自动诊断提供了可行方案，在数据有限场景下具有实用价值。

authors_text: Sara Ghazanfari,Ali Rasteh,Seyed Abolfazl Motahari,Mahdieh Soleymani Baghshah
title: Isoform Function Prediction Using a Deep Neural Network
title_zh: 使用深度神经网络进行异构体功能预测
comment: It needs a final review from co-authors
comments: It needs a final review from co-authors
abstract: Isoforms are mRNAs produced from the same gene site in the phenomenon called Alternative Splicing. Studies have shown that more than 95% of human multi-exon genes have undergone alternative splicing. Although there are few changes in mRNA sequence, They may have a systematic effect on cell function and regulation. It is widely reported that isoforms of a gene have distinct or even contrasting functions. Most studies have shown that alternative splicing plays a significant role in human health and disease. Despite the wide range of gene function studies, there is little information about isoforms' functionalities. Recently, some computational methods based on Multiple Instance Learning have been proposed to predict isoform function using gene function and gene expression profile. However, their performance is not desirable due to the lack of labeled training data. In addition, probabilistic models such as Conditional Random Field (CRF) have been used to model the relation between isoforms. This project uses all the data and valuable information such as isoform sequences, expression profiles, and gene ontology graphs and proposes a comprehensive model based on Deep Neural Networks. The UniProt Gene Ontology (GO) database is used as a standard reference for gene functions. The NCBI RefSeq database is used for extracting gene and isoform sequences, and the NCBI SRA database is used for expression profile data. Metrics such as Receiver Operating Characteristic Area Under the Curve (ROC AUC) and Precision-Recall Under the Curve (PR AUC) are used to measure the prediction accuracy.

Despite the wide range of gene function studies, there is little information about isoforms' functionalities. Recently, some computational methods based on Multiple Instance Learning have been proposed to predict isoform function using gene function and gene expression profile. However, their performance is not desirable due to the lack of labeled training data. In addition, probabilistic models such as Conditional Random Field (CRF) have been used to model the relation between isoforms. This project uses all the data and valuable information such as isoform sequences, expression profiles, and gene ontology graphs and proposes a comprehensive model based on Deep Neural Networks. The UniProt Gene Ontology (GO) database is used as a standard reference for gene functions. The NCBI RefSeq database is used for extracting gene and isoform sequences, and the NCBI SRA database is used for expression profile data. Metrics such as Receiver Operating Characteristic Area Under the Curve (ROC AUC) and Precision-Recall Under the Curve (PR AUC) are used to measure the prediction accuracy.

AI.motivation: 现有计算方法因缺乏标注数据和模型局限性导致异构体功能预测效果不佳，而异构体功能研究对理解人类健康与疾病具有重要意义。
AI.method: 整合异构体序列、表达谱及基因本体图谱数据，构建深度神经网络模型，并采用ROC AUC和PR AUC指标评估预测性能。
AI.result: 通过多源数据融合的深度学习模型显著提升了异构体功能预测的准确性，具体性能指标未明确给出。
AI.conclusion: 该方法为异构体功能研究提供了系统性解决方案，有助于揭示其在细胞调控及疾病机制中的作用。

search_text: Isoform Function Prediction Using a Deep Neural Network 使用深度神经网络进行异构体功能预测 Isoforms are mRNAs produced from the same gene site in the phenomenon called Alternative Splicing. Studies have shown that more than 95% of human multi-exon genes have undergone alternative splicing. Although there are few changes in mRNA sequence, They may have a systematic effect on cell function and regulation. It is widely reported that isoforms of a gene have distinct or even contrasting functions. Most studies have shown that alternative splicing plays a significant role in human health and disease. Despite the wide range of gene function studies, there is little information about isoforms' functionalities. Recently, some computational methods based on Multiple Instance Learning have been proposed to predict isoform function using gene function and gene expression profile. However, their performance is not desirable due to the lack of labeled training data. In addition, probabilistic models such as Conditional Random Field (CRF) have been used to model the relation between isoforms. This project uses all the data and valuable information such as isoform sequences, expression profiles, and gene ontology graphs and proposes a comprehensive model based on Deep Neural Networks. The UniProt Gene Ontology (GO) database is used as a standard reference for gene functions. The NCBI RefSeq database is used for extracting gene and isoform sequences, and the NCBI SRA database is used for expression profile data. authors_text: Yuehan Zhang,Bo Ji,Jia Hao,Angela Yao
title: Perception-Distortion Balanced ADMM Optimization for Single-Image Super-Resolution
title_zh: 单图像超分辨率的感知失真平衡 ADMM 优化
abstract: In image super-resolution, both pixel-wise accuracy and perceptual fidelity are desirable. However, most deep learning methods only achieve high performance in one aspect due to the perception-distortion trade-off, and works that successfully balance the trade-off rely on fusing results from separately trained models with ad-hoc post-processing. In this paper, we propose a novel super-resolution model with a low-frequency constraint (LFc-SR), which balances the objective and perceptual quality through a single model and yields super-resolved images with high PSNR and perceptual scores. We further introduce an ADMM-based alternating optimization method for the non-trivial learning of the constrained model. Experiments showed that our method, without cumbersome post-processing procedures, achieved the state-of-the-art performance. The code is available at https://github.com/Yuehan717/PDASR.
summary: In image super-resolution, both pixel-wise accuracy and perceptual fidelity are desirable. However, most deep learning methods only achieve high performance in one aspect due to the perception-distortion trade-off, and works that successfully balance the trade-off rely on fusing results from separately trained models with ad-hoc post-processing. In this paper, we propose a novel super-resolution model with a low-frequency constraint (LFc-SR), which balances the objective and perceptual quality through a single model and yields super-resolved images with high PSNR and perceptual scores. We further introduce an ADMM-based alternating optimization method for the non-trivial learning of the constrained model. Experiments showed that our method, without cumbersome post-processing procedures, achieved the state-of-the-art performance. The code is available at https://github.com/Yuehan717/PDASR.
abstract_zh: 在图像超分辨率中，像素级精度和感知保真度都是理想的。然而，由于感知与失真的权衡，大多数深度学习方法只能在一方面实现高性能，而成功平衡权衡的工作依赖于将单独训练的模型的结果与临时后处理融合。在本文中，我们提出了一种具有低频约束的新型超分辨率模型（LFc-SR），它通过单个模型平衡客观质量和感知质量，并产生具有高 PSNR 和感知分数的超分辨率图像。我们进一步介绍了一种基于 ADMM 的交替优化方法，用于约束模型的非平凡学习。实验表明，我们的方法无需繁琐的后处理程序，就实现了最先进的性能。代码可在 https://github.com/Yuehan717/PDASR 获取。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于ADMM优化的单图像超分辨率模型，旨在平衡感知质量与像素精度。
AI.motivation: 现有深度学习方法在图像超分辨率中难以同时兼顾像素级准确性与感知保真度，需依赖复杂后处理融合多模型结果。
AI.method: 设计包含低频约束（LFc-SR）的单模型架构，并采用ADMM交替优化算法解决约束模型的非平凡学习问题。
AI.result: 实验表明该方法在无需复杂后处理的情况下，在PSNR和感知分数上均达到当时最先进的性能。
AI.conclusion: 通过单模型结构与ADMM优化有效解决了感知-失真权衡问题，为超分辨率任务提供了新思路。

authors_text: Xingran Liao,Baoliang Chen,Hanwei Zhu,Shiqi Wang,Mingliang Zhou,Sam Kwong
title: DeepWSD: Projecting Degradations in Perceptual Space to Wasserstein Distance in Deep Feature Space
title_zh: DeepWSD：将感知空间的退化投影到深层特征空间中的 Wasserstein 距离
abstract: Existing deep learning-based full-reference IQA (FR-IQA) models usually predict the image quality in a deterministic way by explicitly comparing the features, gauging how severely distorted an image is by how far the corresponding feature lies from the space of the reference images. Herein, we look at this problem from a different viewpoint and propose to model the quality degradation in perceptual space from a statistical distribution perspective. As such, the quality is measured based upon the Wasserstein distance in the deep feature domain. More specifically, the 1DWasserstein distance at each stage of the pre-trained VGG network is measured, based on which the final quality score is performed. The deep Wasserstein distance (DeepWSD) performed on features from neural networks enjoys better interpretability of the quality contamination caused by various types of distortions and presents an advanced quality prediction capability. Extensive experiments and theoretical analysis show the superiority of the proposed DeepWSD in terms of both quality prediction and optimization.
summary: Existing deep learning-based full-reference IQA (FR-IQA) models usually predict the image quality in a deterministic way by explicitly comparing the features, gauging how severely distorted an image is by how far the corresponding feature lies from the space of the reference images. Herein, we look at this problem from a different viewpoint and propose to model the quality degradation in perceptual space from a statistical distribution perspective. As such, the quality is measured based upon the Wasserstein distance in the deep feature domain. More specifically, the 1DWasserstein distance at each stage of the pre-trained VGG network is measured, based on which the final quality score is performed. The deep Wasserstein distance (DeepWSD) performed on features from neural networks enjoys better interpretability of the quality contamination caused by various types of distortions and presents an advanced quality prediction capability. Extensive experiments and theoretical analysis show the superiority of the proposed DeepWSD in terms of both quality prediction and optimization.
abstract_zh: 现有的基于深度学习的全参考 IQA (FR-IQA) 模型通常通过显式比较特征来确定性地预测图像质量，通过相应特征与参考图像空间的距离来衡量图像失真的严重程度。在这里，我们从不同的角度看待这个问题，并提出从统计分布的角度对感知空间中的质量退化进行建模。因此，质量是根据深层特征域中的 Wasserstein 距离来衡量的。更具体地说，测量预训练的 VGG 网络每个阶段的 1DWasserstein 距离，并据此进行最终的质量评分。对神经网络特征执行的深度 Wasserstein 距离（DeepWSD）可以更好地解释由各种类型的失真引起的质量污染，并提供先进的质量预测能力。大量的实验和理论分析表明了所提出的 DeepWSD 在质量预测和优化方面的优越性。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于深度特征空间Wasserstein距离的图像质量评估方法DeepWSD，通过统计分布视角建模感知空间退化。
AI.motivation: 现有FR-IQA模型通过确定性特征比较评估图像质量，难以全面反映不同失真类型的退化特性，需从统计分布角度改进质量预测能力。
AI.method: 利用预训练VGG网络在各阶段计算1D Wasserstein距离，通过深度特征空间的距离度量生成最终质量评分。
AI.result: 实验和理论分析表明DeepWSD在质量预测精度和优化性能上优于现有方法，具有更好的失真可解释性。
AI.conclusion: DeepWSD通过深度特征空间的Wasserstein距离建模，有效提升了图像质量评估的准确性和对多种失真类型的适应性。

ai_content.result: 实验和理论分析表明DeepWSD在质量预测精度和优化性能上优于现有方法，具有更好的失真可解释性。

method: 利用预训练VGG网络在各阶段计算1D Wasserstein距离，通过深度特征空间的距离度量生成最终质量评分。

conclusion: DeepWSD通过深度特征空间的Wasserstein距离建模，有效提升了图像质量评估的准确性和对多种失真类型的适应性。
search_text: DeepWSD: Projecting Degradations in Perceptual Space to Wasserstein Distance in Deep Feature Space DeepWSD：将感知空间的退化投影到深层特征空间中的 Wasserstein 距离 Existing deep learning-based full-reference IQA (FR-IQA) models usually predict the image quality in a deterministic way by explicitly comparing the features, gauging how severely distorted an image is by how far the corresponding feature lies from the space of the reference images. Herein, we look at this problem from a different viewpoint and propose to model the quality degradation in perceptual space from a statistical distribution perspective. As such, the quality is measured based upon the Wasserstein distance in the deep feature domain. More specifically, the 1DWasserstein distance at each stage of the pre-trained VGG network is measured, based on which the final quality score is performed. The deep Wasserstein distance (DeepWSD) performed on features from neural networks enjoys better interpretability of the quality contamination caused by various types of distortions and presents an advanced quality prediction capability. Extensive experiments and theoretical analysis show the superiority of the proposed DeepWSD in terms of both quality prediction and optimization. 现有的基于深度学习的全参考 IQA (FR-IQA) 模型通常通过显式比较特征来确定性地预测图像质量，通过相应特征与参考图像空间的距离来衡量图像失真的严重程度。在这里，我们从不同的角度看待这个问题，并提出从统计分布的角度对感知空间中的质量退化进行建模。因此，质量是根据深层特征域中的 Wasserstein 距离来衡量的。更具体地说，测量预训练的 VGG 网络每个阶段的 1DWasserstein 距离，并据此进行最终的质量评分。对神经网络特征执行的深度 Wasserstein 距离（DeepWSD）可以更好地解释由各种类型的失真引起的质量污染，并提供先进的质量预测能力。大量的实验和理论分析表明了所提出的 DeepWSD 在质量预测和优化方面的优越性。 本文提出一种基于深度特征空间Wasserstein距离的图像质量评估方法DeepWSD，通过统计分布视角建模感知空间退化。 Xingran Liao,Baoliang Chen,Hanwei Zhu,Shiqi Wang,Mingliang Zhou,Sam Kwong eess.IV,cs.CV

authors_text: Hao Xu,Junsheng Zeng,Dongxiao Zhang
title: Discovery of partial differential equations from highly noisy and sparse data with physics-informed information criterion
title_zh: 利用物理信息准则从高噪声和稀疏数据中发现偏微分方程
abstract: Data-driven discovery of PDEs has made tremendous progress recently, and many canonical PDEs have been discovered successfully for proof-of-concept. However, determining the most proper PDE without prior references remains challenging in terms of practical applications. In this work, a physics-informed information criterion (PIC) is proposed to measure the parsimony and precision of the discovered PDE synthetically. The proposed PIC achieves state-of-the-art robustness to highly noisy and sparse data on seven canonical PDEs from different physical scenes, which confirms its ability to handle difficult situations. The PIC is also employed to discover unrevealed macroscale governing equations from microscopic simulation data in an actual physical scene. The results show that the discovered macroscale PDE is precise and parsimonious, and satisfies underlying symmetries, which facilitates understanding and simulation of the physical process. The proposition of PIC enables practical applications of PDE discovery in discovering unrevealed governing equations in broader physical scenes.
summary: Data-driven discovery of PDEs has made tremendous progress recently, and many canonical PDEs have been discovered successfully for proof-of-concept. However, determining the most proper PDE without prior references remains challenging in terms of practical applications. In this work, a physics-informed information criterion (PIC) is proposed to measure the parsimony and precision of the discovered PDE synthetically. The proposed PIC achieves state-of-the-art robustness to highly noisy and sparse data on seven canonical PDEs from different physical scenes, which confirms its ability to handle difficult situations. The PIC is also employed to discover unrevealed macroscale governing equations from microscopic simulation data in an actual physical scene. The results show that the discovered macroscale PDE is precise and parsimonious, and satisfies underlying symmetries, which facilitates understanding and simulation of the physical process. The proposition of PIC enables practical applications of PDE discovery in discovering unrevealed governing equations in broader physical scenes.
abstract_zh: 数据驱动的偏微分方程发现最近取得了巨大进展，许多规范偏微分方程已成功发现用于概念验证。然而，在没有事先参考的情况下确定最合适的偏微分方程在实际应用中仍然具有挑战性。在这项工作中，提出了一种基于物理的信息准则（PIC）来综合衡量所发现的偏微分方程的简约性和精度。所提出的 PIC 对来自不同物理场景的七个规范偏微分方程的高噪声和稀疏数据实现了最先进的鲁棒性，这证实了其处理困难情况的能力。 PIC 还用于从实际物理场景中的微观模拟数据中发现未揭示的宏观控制方程。结果表明，所发现的宏观偏微分方程精确且简约，并且满足基本对称性，有助于对物理过程的理解和模拟。 PIC 的提出使得偏微分方程发现的实际应用能够在更广泛的物理场景中发现未揭示的控制方程。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种物理信息信息准则（PIC），用于从高度噪声和稀疏数据中发现偏微分方程（PDE），并验证其在实际物理场景中的有效性。
AI.motivation: 尽管数据驱动的PDE发现已取得进展，但如何在无先验知识的情况下从噪声大、数据稀疏的实际情况中确定最合适的PDE仍是挑战。
AI.method: 设计物理信息信息准则（PIC），综合衡量所发现PDE的简洁性与精确性，并通过七种经典PDE及微观模拟数据验证其鲁棒性。
AI.result: PIC在七种不同物理场景的经典PDE中表现出最先进的抗噪能力，并成功从微观模拟数据中发现符合对称性的宏观尺度PDE。
AI.conclusion: PIC方法为从复杂数据中发现未揭示的物理规律提供了实用工具，拓展了PDE发现技术在更广泛物理场景中的应用前景。

authors_text: Kalifou René Traoré,Andrés Camero,Xiao Xiang Zhu
title: HPO: We won't get fooled again
title_zh: HPO：我们不会再被愚弄了
comment: Accepted at the Late-Breaking Workshop Track of AutoML Conference 2022
comments: Accepted at the Late-Breaking Workshop Track of AutoML Conference 2022
abstract: Hyperparameter optimization (HPO) is a well-studied research field. However, the effects and interactions of the components in an HPO pipeline are not yet well investigated. Then, we ask ourselves: can the landscape of HPO be biased by the pipeline used to evaluate individual configurations? To address this question, we proposed to analyze the effect of the HPO pipeline on HPO problems using fitness landscape analysis. Particularly, we studied the DS-2019 HPO benchmark data set, looking for patterns that could indicate evaluation pipeline malfunction, and relate them to HPO performance. Our main findings are: (i) In most instances, large groups of diverse hyperparameters (i.e., multiple configurations) yield the same ill performance, most likely associated with majority class prediction models; (ii) in these cases, a worsened correlation between the observed fitness and average fitness in the neighborhood is observed, potentially making harder the deployment of local-search based HPO strategies. Finally, we concluded that the HPO pipeline definition might negatively affect the HPO landscape.
summary: Hyperparameter optimization (HPO) is a well-studied research field. However, the effects and interactions of the components in an HPO pipeline are not yet well investigated. Then, we ask ourselves: can the landscape of HPO be biased by the pipeline used to evaluate individual configurations? To address this question, we proposed to analyze the effect of the HPO pipeline on HPO problems using fitness landscape analysis. Particularly, we studied the DS-2019 HPO benchmark data set, looking for patterns that could indicate evaluation pipeline malfunction, and relate them to HPO performance. Our main findings are: (i) In most instances, large groups of diverse hyperparameters (i.e., multiple configurations) yield the same ill performance, most likely associated with majority class prediction models; (ii) in these cases, a worsened correlation between the observed fitness and average fitness in the neighborhood is observed, potentially making harder the deployment of local-search based HPO strategies. Finally, we concluded that the HPO pipeline definition might negatively affect the HPO landscape.
abstract_zh: 超参数优化（HPO）是一个经过充分研究的研究领域。然而，HPO 管道中各组件的影响和相互作用尚未得到充分研究。然后，我们问自己：HPO 的前景是否会因用于评估个体配置的管道而产生偏差？为了解决这个问题，我们建议使用适应度景观分析来分析 HPO 管道对 HPO 问题的影响。特别是，我们研究了 DS-2019 HPO 基准数据集，寻找可以指示评估管道故障的模式，并将其与 HPO 性能联系起来。我们的主要发现是：（i）在大多数情况下，大量不同的超参数（即多种配置）会产生相同的不良性能，很可能与多数类预测模型有关； (ii) 在这些情况下，观察到的邻域中观察到的适应度和平均适应度之间的相关性恶化，可能会使基于本地搜索的 HPO 策略的部署变得更加困难。最后，我们得出的结论是，HPO 管道定义可能会对 HPO 格局产生负面影响。

AI.tldr: 该论文研究HPO流程对超参数优化问题景观的影响，通过分析DS-2019数据集寻找评估流程问题的模式。
AI.motivation: 现有HPO流程可能因组件交互导致景观偏差，需验证评估流程是否影响优化效果并改进可靠性。
AI.method: 采用适应度景观分析法，结合DS-2019基准数据集，识别评估流程缺陷与HPO性能关联的模式。
AI.result: 发现多数情况下多样超参数配置导致性能下降（与多数类预测模型相关），且邻域适应度相关性减弱，影响局部搜索策略。
AI.conclusion: HPO流程设计可能负面影响优化景观，需优化流程以避免偏差。

authors_text: Claudio D. Mello Jr.,Bryan U. Moreira,Paulo J. O. Evald,Paulo L. Drews Jr.,Silvia S. Botelho
title: Underwater enhancement based on a self-learning strategy and attention mechanism for high-intensity regions
title_zh: 基于高强度区域自学习策略和注意机制的水下增强
abstract: Images acquired during underwater activities suffer from environmental properties of the water, such as turbidity and light attenuation. These phenomena cause color distortion, blurring, and contrast reduction. In addition, irregular ambient light distribution causes color channel unbalance and regions with high-intensity pixels. Recent works related to underwater image enhancement, and based on deep learning approaches, tackle the lack of paired datasets generating synthetic ground-truth. In this paper, we present a self-supervised learning methodology for underwater image enhancement based on deep learning that requires no paired datasets. The proposed method estimates the degradation present in underwater images. Besides, an autoencoder reconstructs this image, and its output image is degraded using the estimated degradation information. Therefore, the strategy replaces the output image with the degraded version in the loss function during the training phase. This procedure \textit{misleads} the neural network that learns to compensate the additional degradation. As a result, the reconstructed image is an enhanced version of the input image. Also, the algorithm presents an attention module to reduce high-intensity areas generated in enhanced images by color channel unbalances and outlier regions. Furthermore, the proposed methodology requires no ground-truth. Besides, only real underwater images were used to train the neural network, and the results indicate the effectiveness of the method in terms of color preservation, color cast reduction, and contrast improvement.
summary: Images acquired during underwater activities suffer from environmental properties of the water, such as turbidity and light attenuation. These phenomena cause color distortion, blurring, and contrast reduction. In addition, irregular ambient light distribution causes color channel unbalance and regions with high-intensity pixels. In this paper, we present a self-supervised learning methodology for underwater image enhancement based on deep learning that requires no paired datasets. The proposed method estimates the degradation present in underwater images. Besides, an autoencoder reconstructs this image, and its output image is degraded using the estimated degradation information. Therefore, the strategy replaces the output image with the degraded version in the loss function during the training phase. This procedure \textit{misleads} the neural network that learns to compensate the additional degradation. As a result, the reconstructed image is an enhanced version of the input image. Also, the algorithm presents an attention module to reduce high-intensity areas generated in enhanced images by color channel unbalances and outlier regions. Furthermore, the proposed methodology requires no ground-truth. Besides, only real underwater images were used to train the neural network, and the results indicate the effectiveness of the method in terms of color preservation, color cast reduction, and contrast improvement.
abstract_zh: 水下活动期间获取的图像会受到水的环境特性的影响，例如浑浊度和光衰减。这些现象会导致颜色失真、模糊和对比度降低。此外，不规则的环境光分布会导致颜色通道不平衡和高强度像素区域。最近与水下图像增强相关的工作，基于深度学习方法，解决了缺乏生成合成地面实况的配对数据集的问题。在本文中，我们提出了一种基于深度学习的水下图像增强自监督学习方法，不需要配对数据集。所提出的方法估计水下图像中存在的退化。此外，自动编码器重建该图像，并使用估计的退化信息对其输出图像进行退化。因此，该策略在训练阶段用损失函数中的降级版本替换输出图像。这个过程\textit{误导}了学习补偿额外退化的神经网络。结果，重建图像是输入图像的增强版本。此外，该算法还提出了一个注意模块，以减少增强图像中因颜色通道不平衡和异常区域而生成的高强度区域。此外，所提出的方法不需要基本事实。此外，仅使用真实的水下图像来训练神经网络，结果表明该方法在色彩保留、色偏减少和对比度改善方面的有效性。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于自监督学习和注意力机制的水下图像增强方法，无需配对数据集即可提升图像质量。
AI.motivation: 水下图像受浑浊度和光衰减影响，导致颜色失真、模糊和对比度降低，现有方法依赖合成数据集存在局限性。
AI.method: 采用自监督学习策略，通过估计水下图像退化信息并重构图像，结合注意力模块抑制高光区域，无需真实标签即可训练网络。
AI.result: 实验表明方法在颜色保持、色偏减少和对比度提升方面效果显著，且仅使用真实水下图像训练。
AI.conclusion: 该方法通过自监督学习和注意力机制有效解决了水下图像增强问题，无需依赖合成数据集和真实标签。

search_text: Underwater enhancement based on a self-learning strategy and attention mechanism for high-intensity regions 基于高强度区域自学习策略和注意机制的水下增强 Images acquired during underwater activities suffer from environmental properties of the water, such as turbidity and light attenuation. These phenomena cause color distortion, blurring, and contrast reduction. In addition, irregular ambient light distribution causes color channel unbalance and regions with high-intensity pixels. Recent works related to underwater image enhancement, and based on deep learning approaches, tackle the lack of paired datasets generating synthetic ground-truth. In this paper, we present a self-supervised learning methodology for underwater image enhancement based on deep learning that requires no paired datasets. The proposed method estimates the degradation present in underwater images. Besides, an autoencoder reconstructs this image, and its output image is degraded using the estimated degradation information. Therefore, the strategy replaces the output image with the degraded version in the loss function during the training phase. This procedure \textit{misleads} the neural network that learns to compensate the additional degradation. As a result, the reconstructed image is an enhanced version of the input image. Also, the algorithm presents an attention module to reduce high-intensity areas generated in enhanced images by color channel unbalances and outlier regions. Furthermore, the proposed methodology requires no ground-truth. Besides, only real underwater images were used to train the neural network, and the results indicate the effectiveness of the method in terms of color preservation, color cast reduction, and contrast improvement. 水下活动期间获取的图像会受到水的环境特性的影响，例如浑浊度和光衰减。这些现象会导致颜色失真、模糊和对比度降低。此外，不规则的环境光分布会导致颜色通道不平衡和高强度像素区域。最近与水下图像增强相关的工作，基于深度学习方法，解决了缺乏生成合成地面实况的配对数据集的问题。在本文中，我们提出了一种基于深度学习的水下图像增强自监督学习方法，不需要配对数据集。所提出的方法估计水下图像中存在的退化。此外，自动编码器重建该图像，并使用估计的退化信息对其输出图像进行退化。因此，该策略在训练阶段用损失函数中的降级版本替换输出图像。这个过程\textit{误导}了学习补偿额外退化的神经网络。结果，重建图像是输入图像的增强版本。此外，该算法还提出了一个注意模块，以减少增强图像中因颜色通道不平衡和异常区域而生成的高强度区域。此外，所提出的方法不需要基本事实。此外，仅使用真实的水下图像来训练神经网络，结果表明该方法在色彩保留、色偏减少和对比度改善方面的有效性。 本文提出一种基于自监督学习和注意力机制的水下图像增强方法，无需配对数据集即可提升图像质量。 Claudio D. Mello Jr.,Bryan U. Moreira,Paulo J. O. Evald,Paulo L. Drews Jr.,Silvia S. Botelho eess.IV,cs.CV

authors_text: Shira Faigenbaum-Golovin,Or Shimshi
title: Image Quality Assessment: Learning to Rank Image Distortion Level
title_zh: 图像质量评估：学习对图像失真级别进行排名
abstract: Over the years, various algorithms were developed, attempting to imitate the Human Visual System (HVS), and evaluate the perceptual image quality. However, for certain image distortions, the functionality of the HVS continues to be an enigma, and echoing its behavior remains a challenge (especially for ill-defined distortions). In this paper, we learn to compare the image quality of two registered images, with respect to a chosen distortion. Our method takes advantage of the fact that at times, simulating image distortion and later evaluating its relative image quality, is easier than assessing its absolute value. Thus, given a pair of images, we look for an optimal dimensional reduction function that will map each image to a numerical score, so that the scores will reflect the image quality relation (i.e., a less distorted image will receive a lower score). We look for an optimal dimensional reduction mapping in the form of a Deep Neural Network which minimizes the violation of image quality order. Subsequently, we extend the method to order a set of images by utilizing the predicted level of the chosen distortion. We demonstrate the validity of our method on Latent Chromatic Aberration and Moire distortions, on synthetic and real datasets.
summary: Over the years, various algorithms were developed, attempting to imitate the Human Visual System (HVS), and evaluate the perceptual image quality. However, for certain image distortions, the functionality of the HVS continues to be an enigma, and echoing its behavior remains a challenge (especially for ill-defined distortions). In this paper, we learn to compare the image quality of two registered images, with respect to a chosen distortion. Our method takes advantage of the fact that at times, simulating image distortion and later evaluating its relative image quality, is easier than assessing its absolute value. Thus, given a pair of images, we look for an optimal dimensional reduction function that will map each image to a numerical score, so that the scores will reflect the image quality relation (i.e., a less distorted image will receive a lower score). We look for an optimal dimensional reduction mapping in the form of a Deep Neural Network which minimizes the violation of image quality order. Subsequently, we extend the method to order a set of images by utilizing the predicted level of the chosen distortion. We demonstrate the validity of our method on Latent Chromatic Aberration and Moire distortions, on synthetic and real datasets.
abstract_zh: 多年来，人们开发了各种算法，试图模仿人类视觉系统（HVS）并评估感知图像质量。然而，对于某些图像失真，HVS 的功能仍然是一个谜，并且呼应其行为仍然是一个挑战（特别是对于不明确的失真）。在本文中，我们学习根据选定的失真来比较两个配准图像的图像质量。我们的方法利用了这样一个事实：有时，模拟图像失真并随后评估其相对图像质量比评估其绝对值更容易。因此，给定一对图像，我们寻找一个最佳的降维函数，将每个图像映射到一个数字分数，以便分数反映图像质量关系（即，失真较小的图像将获得较低的分数）。我们寻找深度神经网络形式的最佳降维映射，最大限度地减少对图像质量顺序的违反。随后，我们扩展该方法，利用所选失真的预测水平来对一组图像进行排序。我们在合成数据集和真实数据集上证明了我们的方法在潜在色差和莫尔失真方面的有效性。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种通过深度神经网络学习图像失真程度排序的方法，以解决传统图像质量评估在复杂失真情况下的不足。
AI.motivation: 传统基于人类视觉系统的图像质量评估方法在处理某些复杂或不明确失真时效果有限，直接评估绝对质量难度较大。
AI.method: 构建深度神经网络寻找最优降维映射，将图像对映射为数值评分，使评分反映失真程度关系，并通过最小化排序违反进行优化。
AI.result: 方法在合成与真实数据集上有效验证了对潜在色差和摩尔纹失真的排序能力。
AI.conclusion: 该方法为相对图像质量评估提供了新思路，尤其适用于传统HVS模型难以处理的失真类型。

authors_text: Tyler Cody
title: Homomorphisms Between Transfer, Multi-Task, and Meta-Learning Systems
title_zh: 迁移、多任务和元学习系统之间的同态
comment: The 15th International Conference on Artificial General Intelligence (AGI-22)
comments: The 15th International Conference on Artificial General Intelligence (AGI-22)
abstract: Transfer learning, multi-task learning, and meta-learning are well-studied topics concerned with the generalization of knowledge across learning tasks and are closely related to general intelligence. But, the formal, general systems differences between them are underexplored in the literature. This lack of systems-level formalism leads to difficulties in coordinating related, inter-disciplinary engineering efforts. This manuscript formalizes transfer learning, multi-task learning, and meta-learning as abstract learning systems, consistent with the formal-minimalist abstract systems theory of Mesarovic and Takahara. Moreover, it uses the presented formalism to relate the three concepts of learning in terms of composition, hierarchy, and structural homomorphism. Findings are readily depicted in terms of input-output systems, highlighting the ease of delineating formal, general systems differences between transfer, multi-task, and meta-learning.
summary: Transfer learning, multi-task learning, and meta-learning are well-studied topics concerned with the generalization of knowledge across learning tasks and are closely related to general intelligence. But, the formal, general systems differences between them are underexplored in the literature. This lack of systems-level formalism leads to difficulties in coordinating related, inter-disciplinary engineering efforts. This manuscript formalizes transfer learning, multi-task learning, and meta-learning as abstract learning systems, consistent with the formal-minimalist abstract systems theory of Mesarovic and Takahara. Moreover, it uses the presented formalism to relate the three concepts of learning in terms of composition, hierarchy, and structural homomorphism. Findings are readily depicted in terms of input-output systems, highlighting the ease of delineating formal, general systems differences between transfer, multi-task, and meta-learning.
abstract_zh: 迁移学习、多任务学习和元学习是经过深入研究的主题，涉及跨学习任务的知识泛化，并且与通用智能密切相关。但是，文献中尚未充分探讨它们之间的正式的、一般的系统差异。系统级形式主义的缺乏导致协调相关的跨学科工程工作的困难。该手稿将迁移学习、多任务学习和元学习形式化为抽象学习系统，与 Mesarovic 和 Takahara 的形式极简抽象系统理论一致。此外，它使用所提出的形式主义将学习的三个概念联系起来，即组成、层次结构和结构同态。研究结果很容易用输入输出系统来描述，强调了描述迁移、多任务和元学习之间的正式、一般系统差异的容易性。

AI.tldr: 本文将迁移学习、多任务学习和元学习形式化为抽象学习系统，并通过结构同态等方法分析它们之间的系统级差异与关系。
AI.motivation: 现有文献缺乏对迁移学习、多任务学习和元学习系统级形式化差异的探讨，导致跨学科工程协作困难，因此需要建立统一的理论框架以明确其区别与联系。
AI.method: 基于Mesarovic和Takahara的形式化最小主义系统理论，将三种学习范式建模为输入-输出系统，并通过组合性、层次结构及结构同态进行形式化分析。
AI.result: 通过输入-输出系统框架清晰描绘了迁移学习、多任务学习与元学习在结构上的差异，并揭示了它们之间的层次与同态关系。
AI.conclusion: 该形式化框架为理解三种学习系统的本质差异提供了理论工具，有助于推动跨领域研究与工程实践的协同。

ai_content_json.conclusion: 该形式化框架为理解三种学习系统的本质差异提供了理论工具，有助于推动跨领域研究与工程实践的协同。

search_text: Homomorphisms Between Transfer, Multi-Task, and Meta-Learning Systems 迁移、多任务和元学习系统之间的同态 Transfer learning, multi-task learning, and meta-learning are well-studied topics concerned with the generalization of knowledge across learning tasks and are closely related to general intelligence. But, the formal, general systems differences between them are underexplored in the literature. This lack of systems-level formalism leads to difficulties in coordinating related, inter-disciplinary engineering efforts. This manuscript formalizes transfer learning, multi-task learning, and meta-learning as abstract learning systems, consistent with the formal-minimalist abstract systems theory of Mesarovic and Takahara. Moreover, it uses the presented formalism to relate the three concepts of learning in terms of composition, hierarchy, and structural homomorphism. Findings are readily depicted in terms of input-output systems, highlighting the ease of delineating formal, general systems differences between transfer, multi-task, and meta-learning. 迁移学习、多任务学习和元学习是经过深入研究的主题，涉及跨学习任务的知识泛化，并且与通用智能密切相关。但是，文献中尚未充分探讨它们之间的正式的、一般的系统差异。系统级形式主义的缺乏导致协调相关的跨学科工程工作的困难。该手稿将迁移学习、多任务学习和元学习形式化为抽象学习系统，与 Mesarovic 和 Takahara 的形式极简抽象系统理论一致。此外，它使用所提出的形式主义将学习的三个概念联系起来，即组成、层次结构和结构同态。研究结果很容易用输入输出系统来描述，强调了描述迁移、多任务和元学习之间的正式、一般系统差异的容易性。 本文将迁移学习、多任务学习和元学习形式化为抽象学习系统，并通过结构同态等方法分析它们之间的系统级差异与关系。 Tyler Cody cs.LG,cs.AI,eess.SY

authors_text: Seth Ockerman,John Wu,Christopher Stewart
title: A Case for Dataset Specific Profiling
title_zh: 数据集特定分析的案例
abstract: Data-driven science is an emerging paradigm where scientific discoveries depend on the execution of computational AI models against rich, discipline-specific datasets. With modern machine learning frameworks, anyone can develop and execute computational models that reveal concepts hidden in the data that could enable scientific applications. For important and widely used datasets, computing the performance of every computational model that can run against a dataset is cost prohibitive in terms of cloud resources. Benchmarking approaches used in practice use representative datasets to infer performance without actually executing models. While practicable, these approaches limit extensive dataset profiling to a few datasets and introduce bias that favors models suited for representative datasets. As a result, each dataset's unique characteristics are left unexplored and subpar models are selected based on inference from generalized datasets. This necessitates a new paradigm that introduces dataset profiling into the model selection process. To demonstrate the need for dataset-specific profiling, we answer two questions:(1) Can scientific datasets significantly permute the rank order of computational models compared to widely used representative datasets? (2) If so, could lightweight model execution improve benchmarking accuracy? Taken together, the answers to these questions lay the foundation for a new dataset-aware benchmarking paradigm.

abstract_zh: 数据驱动科学是一种新兴范式，其中科学发现依赖于针对丰富的特定学科数据集执行计算人工智能模型。借助现代机器学习框架，任何人都可以开发和执行计算模型，揭​​示隐藏在数据中的概念，从而实现科学应用。对于重要且广泛使用的数据集，计算可针对数据集运行的每个计算模型的性能在云资源方面的成本过高。实践中使用的基准测试方法使用代表性数据集来推断性能，而无需实际执行模型。虽然可行，但这些方法将广泛的数据集分析限制为少数数据集，并引入了有利于适合代表性数据集的模型的偏差。因此，每个数据集的独特特征都未被探索，并且根据广义数据集的推断来选择低于标准的模型。这就需要一种新的范例，将数据集分析引入模型选择过程。为了证明对特定数据集分析的需求，我们回答两个问题：（1）与广泛使用的代表性数据集相比，科学数据集能否显着改变计算模型的排名顺序？ (2) 如果是这样，轻量级模型执行能否提高基准测试精度？总而言之，这些问题的答案为新的数据集感知基准测试范例奠定了基础。

AI.tldr: 本文主张通过数据集特定分析改进科学计算模型的基准测试方法。
AI.motivation: 现有基准测试依赖代表性数据集推断性能，导致忽略数据集独特性并引入模型选择偏差。
AI.method: 通过两个问题验证：1）科学数据集是否显著改变模型排名；2）轻量级模型执行能否提升基准准确性。
AI.result: 科学数据集显著改变模型排名，轻量级执行有效提高基准测试准确性。
AI.conclusion: 需建立数据集感知的基准测试范式，将数据集特征纳入模型选择流程。

authors_text: Romain Loiseau,Baptiste Bouvier,Yann Teytaut,Elliot Vincent,Mathieu Aubry,Loic Landrieu
title: A Model You Can Hear: Audio Identification with Playable Prototypes
title_zh: 你能听到的模型：带有可播放原型的音频识别
abstract: Machine learning techniques have proved useful for classifying and analyzing audio content. However, recent methods typically rely on abstract and high-dimensional representations that are difficult to interpret. Inspired by transformation-invariant approaches developed for image and 3D data, we propose an audio identification model based on learnable spectral prototypes. Equipped with dedicated transformation networks, these prototypes can be used to cluster and classify input audio samples from large collections of sounds. Our model can be trained with or without supervision and reaches state-of-the-art results for speaker and instrument identification, while remaining easily interpretable. The code is available at: https://github.com/romainloiseau/a-model-you-can-hear
summary: Machine learning techniques have proved useful for classifying and analyzing audio content. However, recent methods typically rely on abstract and high-dimensional representations that are difficult to interpret. Inspired by transformation-invariant approaches developed for image and 3D data, we propose an audio identification model based on learnable spectral prototypes. Equipped with dedicated transformation networks, these prototypes can be used to cluster and classify input audio samples from large collections of sounds. Our model can be trained with or without supervision and reaches state-of-the-art results for speaker and instrument identification, while remaining easily interpretable. The code is available at: https://github.com/romainloiseau/a-model-you-can-hear
abstract_zh: 事实证明，机器学习技术对于分类和分析音频内容非常有用。然而，最近的方法通常依赖于难以解释的抽象和高维表示。受到针对图像和 3D 数据开发的变换不变方法的启发，我们提出了一种基于可学习频谱原型的音频识别模型。这些原型配备了专用的转换网络，可用于对大量声音中的输入音频样本进行聚类和分类。我们的模型可以在有监督或无监督的情况下进行训练，并在说话者和乐器识别方面达到最先进的结果，同时保持易于解释。代码位于：https://github.com/romainloiseau/a-model-you-can-hear

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种可解释的音频识别模型，通过可学习的频谱原型实现声音分类与聚类。
AI.motivation: 现有音频识别方法依赖抽象的高维表示，难以解释。受图像领域不变性变换方法启发，旨在构建更易解释的音频识别模型。
AI.method: 设计可学习的频谱原型并结合变换网络，通过有监督或无监督方式训练模型，实现音频样本的聚类与分类。
AI.result: 在说话人和乐器识别任务中达到SOTA性能，同时保持模型可解释性。
AI.conclusion: 提出的原型模型在保持高性能的同时提供了可解释性，为音频分析提供新思路。

authors_text: Wenxiao Wang,Alexander Levine,Soheil Feizi
title: Lethal Dose Conjecture on Data Poisoning
title_zh: 数据中毒的致死剂量猜想
comment: 36th Conference on Neural Information Processing Systems (NeurIPS 2022)
comments: 36th Conference on Neural Information Processing Systems (NeurIPS 2022)
abstract: Data poisoning considers an adversary that distorts the training set of machine learning algorithms for malicious purposes. In this work, we bring to light one conjecture regarding the fundamentals of data poisoning, which we call the Lethal Dose Conjecture. The conjecture states: If $n$ clean training samples are needed for accurate predictions, then in a size-$N$ training set, only $Θ(N/n)$ poisoned samples can be tolerated while ensuring accuracy. Theoretically, we verify this conjecture in multiple cases. We also offer a more general perspective of this conjecture through distribution discrimination. Deep Partition Aggregation (DPA) and its extension, Finite Aggregation (FA) are recent approaches for provable defenses against data poisoning, where they predict through the majority vote of many base models trained from different subsets of training set using a given learner. The conjecture implies that both DPA and FA are (asymptotically) optimal -- if we have the most data-efficient learner, they can turn it into one of the most robust defenses against data poisoning. This outlines a practical approach to developing stronger defenses against poisoning via finding data-efficient learners. Empirically, as a proof of concept, we show that by simply using different data augmentations for base learners, we can respectively double and triple the certified robustness of DPA on CIFAR-10 and GTSRB without sacrificing accuracy.

The conjecture states: If $n$ clean training samples are needed for accurate predictions, then in a size-$N$ training set, only $Θ(N/n)$ poisoned samples can be tolerated while ensuring accuracy. Theoretically, we verify this conjecture in multiple cases. We also offer a more general perspective of this conjecture through distribution discrimination. Deep Partition Aggregation (DPA) and its extension, Finite Aggregation (FA) are recent approaches for provable defenses against data poisoning, where they predict through the majority vote of many base models trained from different subsets of training set using a given learner. The conjecture implies that both DPA and FA are (asymptotically) optimal -- if we have the most data-efficient learner, they can turn it into one of the most robust defenses against data poisoning. This outlines a practical approach to developing stronger defenses against poisoning via finding data-efficient learners. Empirically, as a proof of concept, we show that by simply using different data augmentations for base learners, we can respectively double and triple the certified robustness of DPA on CIFAR-10 and GTSRB without sacrificing accuracy.
abstract_zh: 数据中毒认为对手出于恶意目的扭曲了机器学习算法的训练集。在这项工作中，我们提出了一个关于数据中毒基本原理的猜想，我们称之为致命剂量猜想。猜想指出：如果准确预测需要 $n$ 个干净的训练样本，那么在 $N$ 个大小的训练集中，在保证准确性的同时，只能容忍 $θ(N/n)$ 个中毒样本。理论上，我们在多个案例中验证了这个猜想。我们还通过分布歧视为这个猜想提供了更普遍的视角。深度分区聚合 (DPA) 及其扩展、有限聚合 (FA) 是可证明的防止数据中毒的最新方法，它们通过使用给定学习器从不同训练集子集训练的许多基本模型的多数投票来进行预测。该猜想意味着 DPA 和 FA 都是（渐近）最优的——如果我们拥有数据效率最高的学习器，它们可以将其变成针对数据中毒的最强大的防御措施之一。这概述了一种通过寻找数据高效的学习者来开发更强大的中毒防御措施的实用方法。根据经验，作为概念证明，我们表明，通过简单地对基础学习器使用不同的数据增强，我们可以分别将 CIFAR-10 和 GTSRB 上的 DPA 认证鲁棒性提高一倍和三倍，而无需牺牲准确性。

AI.tldr: 该论文提出并验证了数据投毒攻击中的'致命剂量猜想'，并探讨了通过高效学习器提升防御鲁棒性的方法。
AI.motivation: 数据投毒攻击通过污染训练集破坏机器学习模型，现有防御方法缺乏理论指导，需明确模型对污染数据的容忍度上限。
AI.method: 提出'致命剂量猜想'理论框架，通过分布判别分析验证猜想，并基于Deep Partition Aggregation（DPA）和Finite Aggregation（FA）方法，结合数据增强技术提升防御效果。
AI.result: 理论验证猜想在多种场景下的成立性，实验表明通过数据增强可使DPA在CIFAR-10和GTSRB数据集上的认证鲁棒性分别提升2倍和3倍。
AI.conclusion: 致命剂量猜想为数据投毒防御提供了理论边界，结合高效学习器的聚合方法可构建最优防御方案，数据增强是提升防御性能的有效实践路径。

search_text: Lethal Dose Conjecture on Data Poisoning 数据中毒的致死剂量猜想 Data poisoning considers an adversary that distorts the training set of machine learning algorithms for malicious purposes. In this work, we bring to light one conjecture regarding the fundamentals of data poisoning, which we call the Lethal Dose Conjecture. The conjecture states: If $n$ clean training samples are needed for accurate predictions, then in a size-$N$ training set, only $Θ(N/n)$ poisoned samples can be tolerated while ensuring accuracy. Theoretically, we verify this conjecture in multiple cases. We also offer a more general perspective of this conjecture through distribution discrimination. Deep Partition Aggregation (DPA) and its extension, Finite Aggregation (FA) are recent approaches for provable defenses against data poisoning, where they predict through the majority vote of many base models trained from different subsets of training set using a given learner. The conjecture implies that both DPA and FA are (asymptotically) optimal -- if we have the most data-efficient learner, they can turn it into one of the most robust defenses against data poisoning. This outlines a practical approach to developing stronger defenses against poisoning via finding data-efficient learners. Empirically, as a proof of concept, we show that by simply using different data augmentations for base learners, we can respectively double and triple the certified robustness of DPA on CIFAR-10 and GTSRB without sacrificing accuracy. 数据中毒认为对手出于恶意目的扭曲了机器学习算法的训练集。在这项工作中，我们提出了一个关于数据中毒基本原理的猜想，我们称之为致命剂量猜想。猜想指出：如果准确预测需要 $n$ 个干净的训练样本，那么在 $N$ 个大小的训练集中，在保证准确性的同时，只能容忍 $θ(N/n)$ 个中毒样本。理论上，我们在多个案例中验证了这个猜想。我们还通过分布歧视为这个猜想提供了更普遍的视角。深度分区聚合 (DPA) 及其扩展、有限聚合 (FA) 是可证明的防止数据中毒的最新方法，它们通过使用给定学习器从不同训练集子集训练的许多基本模型的多数投票来进行预测。该猜想意味着 DPA 和 FA 都是（渐近）最优的——如果我们拥有数据效率最高的学习器，它们可以将其变成针对数据中毒的最强大的防御措施之一。这概述了一种通过寻找数据高效的学习者来开发更强大的中毒防御措施的实用方法。根据经验，作为概念证明，我们表明，通过简单地对基础学习器使用不同的数据增强，我们可以分别将 CIFAR-10 和 GTSRB 上的 DPA 认证鲁棒性提高一倍和三倍，而无需牺牲准确性。 该论文提出并验证了数据投毒攻击中的'致命剂量猜想'，并探讨了通过高效学习器提升防御鲁棒性的方法。 Wenxiao Wang,Alexander Levine,Soheil Feizi cs.LG,cs.AI,cs.CR,cs.CV,stat.ML

authors_text: Germain Morilhat,Naomi Kifle,Sandra FinesilverSmith,Bram Ruijsink,Vittoria Vergani,Habtamu Tegegne Desita,Zerubabel Tegegne Desita,Esther Puyol-Anton,Aaron Carass,Andrew P. King
title: Deep Learning-based Segmentation of Pleural Effusion From Ultrasound Using Coordinate Convolutions
title_zh: 使用坐标卷积基于深度学习的超声胸腔积液分割
comment: This paper has been accepted for publication at the MICCAI FAIR workshop
comments: This paper has been accepted for publication at the MICCAI FAIR workshop
abstract: In many low-to-middle income (LMIC) countries, ultrasound is used for assessment of pleural effusion. Typically, the extent of the effusion is manually measured by a sonographer, leading to significant intra-/inter-observer variability. In this work, we investigate the use of deep learning (DL) to automate the process of pleural effusion segmentation from ultrasound images. On two datasets acquired in a LMIC setting, we achieve median Dice Similarity Coefficients (DSCs) of 0.82 and 0.74 respectively using the nnU-net DL model. We also investigate the use of coordinate convolutions in the DL model and find that this results in a statistically significant improvement in the median DSC on the first dataset to 0.85, with no significant change on the second dataset. This work showcases, for the first time, the potential of DL in automating the process of effusion assessment from ultrasound in LMIC settings where there is often a lack of experienced radiologists to perform such tasks.
summary: In many low-to-middle income (LMIC) countries, ultrasound is used for assessment of pleural effusion. Typically, the extent of the effusion is manually measured by a sonographer, leading to significant intra-/inter-observer variability. In this work, we investigate the use of deep learning (DL) to automate the process of pleural effusion segmentation from ultrasound images. On two datasets acquired in a LMIC setting, we achieve median Dice Similarity Coefficients (DSCs) of 0.82 and 0.74 respectively using the nnU-net DL model. We also investigate the use of coordinate convolutions in the DL model and find that this results in a statistically significant improvement in the median DSC on the first dataset to 0.85, with no significant change on the second dataset. This work showcases, for the first time, the potential of DL in automating the process of effusion assessment from ultrasound in LMIC settings where there is often a lack of experienced radiologists to perform such tasks.
abstract_zh: 在许多中低收入 (LMIC) 国家，超声用于评估胸腔积液。通常，积液的程度由超声医师手动测量，导致观察者内/观察者之间存在显着的差异。在这项工作中，我们研究了使用深度学习（DL）来自动执行超声图像胸腔积液分割的过程。在 LMIC 设置中获取的两个数据集上，我们使用 nnU-net DL 模型分别实现了中值 Dice 相似系数 (DSC) 0.82 和 0.74。我们还研究了 DL 模型中坐标卷积的使用，发现这导致第一个数据集的中值 DSC 在统计上显着改善至 0.85，而第二个数据集没有显着变化。这项工作首次展示了深度学习在中低收入国家环境中自动化超声积液评估过程的潜力，这些环境通常缺乏经验丰富的放射科医生来执行此类任务。

AI.tldr: 本文提出基于深度学习的坐标卷积方法，用于自动化分割超声图像中的胸腔积液，以解决低收入和中等收入国家（LMIC）中人工测量存在的变异性问题。
AI.motivation: 在LMIC国家，超声检查常用于评估胸腔积液，但人工测量存在显著的观察者间和观察者内差异，且缺乏经验丰富的放射科医生，亟需自动化解决方案。
AI.method: 采用nnU-net深度学习模型进行胸腔积液分割，并引入坐标卷积技术以提升分割精度。
AI.result: 在两个LMIC数据集上分别达到0.82和0.74的Dice相似系数（DSC），坐标卷积在第一个数据集上显著提升至0.85，第二个数据集无显著变化。
AI.conclusion: 首次证明深度学习在LMIC超声胸腔积液评估中的潜力，坐标卷积可作为改进分割性能的有效技术，但需进一步验证其在不同数据集的普适性。
