authors_text: Da Ju,Jing Xu,Y-Lan Boureau,Jason Weston
title: Learning from data in the mixed adversarial non-adversarial case: Finding the helpers and ignoring the trolls
title_zh: 从混合对抗性非对抗性案例中的数据中学习：寻找帮助者并忽略巨魔
abstract: The promise of interaction between intelligent conversational agents and humans is that models can learn from such feedback in order to improve. Unfortunately, such exchanges in the wild will not always involve human utterances that are benign or of high quality, and will include a mixture of engaged (helpers) and unengaged or even malicious users (trolls). In this work we study how to perform robust learning in such an environment. We introduce a benchmark evaluation, SafetyMix, which can evaluate methods that learn safe vs. toxic language in a variety of adversarial settings to test their robustness. We propose and analyze several mitigating learning algorithms that identify trolls either at the example or at the user level. Our main finding is that user-based methods, that take into account that troll users will exhibit adversarial behavior across multiple examples, work best in a variety of settings on our benchmark. We then test these methods in a further real-life setting of conversations collected during deployment, with similar results.
summary: The promise of interaction between intelligent conversational agents and humans is that models can learn from such feedback in order to improve. Unfortunately, such exchanges in the wild will not always involve human utterances that are benign or of high quality, and will include a mixture of engaged (helpers) and unengaged or even malicious users (trolls). In this work we study how to perform robust learning in such an environment. We introduce a benchmark evaluation, SafetyMix, which can evaluate methods that learn safe vs. toxic language in a variety of adversarial settings to test their robustness. We propose and analyze several mitigating learning algorithms that identify trolls either at the example or at the user level. Our main finding is that user-based methods, that take into account that troll users will exhibit adversarial behavior across multiple examples, work best in a variety of settings on our benchmark. We then test these methods in a further real-life setting of conversations collected during deployment, with similar results.
abstract_zh: 智能会话代理与人类之间交互的前景是模型可以从此类反馈中学习以进行改进。不幸的是，这种野外交流并不总是涉及良性或高质量的人类话语，并且将包括参与的（帮助者）和不参与的甚至恶意的用户（巨魔）的混合。在这项工作中，我们研究如何在这样的环境中进行稳健的学习。我们引入了基准评估 SafetyMix，它可以评估在各种对抗环境中学习安全语言与有毒语言的方法，以测试其稳健性。我们提出并分析了几种缓解学习算法，这些算法可以在示例或用户级别识别巨魔。我们的主要发现是基于用户的方法，考虑到巨魔用户将在多个示例中表现出对抗行为，在我们的基准测试的各种设置中效果最佳。然后，我们在部署过程中收集的对话的进一步现实生活环境中测试这些方法，得到类似的结果。

AI.tldr: 本文研究在存在恶意用户（trolls）和良性用户（helpers）的混合环境中，如何实现稳健的模型学习。
AI.motivation: 实际交互中用户反馈质量参差不齐，恶意用户可能破坏模型训练，需开发能区分并过滤恶意输入的鲁棒学习方法。
AI.method: 提出SafetyMix基准测试评估对抗环境下的学习方法，并设计用户级/示例级算法识别恶意用户，重点分析跨多示例的用户行为模式。
AI.result: 用户级方法在基准测试和真实对话场景中均表现最优，能有效识别恶意用户并提升模型鲁棒性。

search_text: Learning from data in the mixed adversarial non-adversarial case: Finding the helpers and ignoring the trolls 从混合对抗性非对抗性案例中的数据中学习：寻找帮助者并忽略巨魔 The promise of interaction between intelligent conversational agents and humans is that models can learn from such feedback in order to improve. Unfortunately, such exchanges in the wild will not always involve human utterances that are benign or of high quality, and will include a mixture of engaged (helpers) and unengaged or even malicious users (trolls). In this work we study how to perform robust learning in such an environment. We introduce a benchmark evaluation, SafetyMix, which can evaluate methods that learn safe vs. toxic language in a variety of adversarial settings to test their robustness. We propose and analyze several mitigating learning algorithms that identify trolls either at the example or at the user level. Our main finding is that user-based methods, that take into account that troll users will exhibit adversarial behavior across multiple examples, work best in a variety of settings on our benchmark. We then test these methods in a further real-life setting of conversations collected during deployment, with similar results. 智能会话代理与人类之间交互的前景是模型可以从此类反馈中学习以进行改进。不幸的是，这种野外交流并不总是涉及良性或高质量的人类话语，并且将包括参与的（帮助者）和不参与的甚至恶意的用户（巨魔）的混合。在这项工作中，我们研究如何在这样的环境中进行稳健的学习。我们引入了基准评估 SafetyMix，它可以评估在各种对抗环境中学习安全语言与有毒语言的方法，以测试其稳健性。我们提出并分析了几种缓解学习算法，这些算法可以在示例或用户级别识别巨魔。我们的主要发现是基于用户的方法，考虑到巨魔用户将在多个示例中表现出对抗行为，在我们的基准测试的各种设置中效果最佳。然后，我们在部署过程中收集的对话的进一步现实生活环境中测试这些方法，得到类似的结果。 本文研究在存在恶意用户（trolls）和良性用户（helpers）的混合环境中，如何实现稳健的模型学习。 Da Ju,Jing Xu,Y-Lan Boureau,Jason Weston cs.CL,cs.AI

authors_text: Mingyang Gong,Brett Edgar,Jing Fan,Guohui Lin,Eiji Miyano
title: Approximation algorithms for covering vertices by long paths
title_zh: 通过长路径覆盖顶点的近似算法
comment: 27 pages; an extended abstract appears in MFCS 2022
comments: 27 pages; an extended abstract appears in MFCS 2022
abstract: Given a graph, the general problem to cover the maximum number of vertices by a collection of vertex-disjoint long paths seemingly escapes from the literature. A path containing at least $k$ vertices is considered long. When $k \le 3$, the problem is polynomial time solvable; when $k$ is the total number of vertices, the problem reduces to the Hamiltonian path problem, which is NP-complete. For a fixed $k \ge 4$, the problem is NP-hard and the best known approximation algorithm for the weighted set packing problem implies a $k$-approximation algorithm. To the best of our knowledge, there is no approximation algorithm directly designed for the general problem; when $k = 4$, the problem admits a $4$-approximation algorithm which was presented recently. We propose the first $(0.4394 k + O(1))$-approximation algorithm for the general problem and an improved $2$-approximation algorithm when $k = 4$. Both algorithms are based on local improvement, and their theoretical performance analyses are done via amortization and their practical performance is examined through simulation studies.
summary: Given a graph, the general problem to cover the maximum number of vertices by a collection of vertex-disjoint long paths seemingly escapes from the literature. A path containing at least $k$ vertices is considered long. When $k \le 3$, the problem is polynomial time solvable; when $k$ is the total number of vertices, the problem reduces to the Hamiltonian path problem, which is NP-complete. For a fixed $k \ge 4$, the problem is NP-hard and the best known approximation algorithm for the weighted set packing problem implies a $k$-approximation algorithm. To the best of our knowledge, there is no approximation algorithm directly designed for the general problem; when $k = 4$, the problem admits a $4$-approximation algorithm which was presented recently. We propose the first $(0.4394 k + O(1))$-approximation algorithm for the general problem and an improved $2$-approximation algorithm when $k = 4$. Both algorithms are based on local improvement, and their theoretical performance analyses are done via amortization and their practical performance is examined through simulation studies.
abstract_zh: 给定一个图，通过一组顶点不相交的长路径来覆盖最大数量的顶点的一般问题似乎从文献中逃脱了。包含至少 $k$ 个顶点的路径被认为是长的。当$k \le 3$时，问题是多项式时间可解的；当$k$是顶点总数时，问题简化为哈密顿路径问题，这是NP完全的。对于固定的 $k \ge 4$，该问题是 NP 困难的，并且加权集打包问题的最著名的近似算法意味着 $k$ 近似算法。据我们所知，还没有直接针对一般问题设计的近似算法；当$k = 4$时，该问题承认最近提出的$4$近似算法。我们针对一般问题提出了第一个 $(0.4394 k + O(1))$ 近似算法，并在 $k = 4$ 时提出了改进的 $2$ 近似算法。两种算法均基于局部改进，其理论性能分析通过摊销完成，其实际性能通过仿真研究检验。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种针对图中用长路径覆盖顶点问题的近似算法，解决了当路径长度k≥4时的NP难问题。
AI.motivation: 现有算法在k≤3时可多项式求解，但对一般k≥4的情况缺乏直接设计的近似算法，且k=4时仅存在4-近似算法。
AI.method: 基于局部改进策略，结合摊还分析法设计近似算法，并通过仿真实验验证实际性能。
AI.result: 提出首个(0.4394k + O(1))-近似算法，并改进k=4时的2-近似算法，理论分析与实验验证均有效。
AI.conclusion: 新算法在理论保证和实际表现上均优于现有方法，为长路径覆盖问题提供了更高效的解决方案。

authors_text: Soumyajit Karmakar,Abeer Banerjee,Prashant Sadashiv Gidde,Sumeet Saurav,Sanjay Singh
title: Convolutional Ensembling based Few-Shot Defect Detection Technique
title_zh: 基于卷积集成的少样本缺陷检测技术
abstract: Over the past few years, there has been a significant improvement in the domain of few-shot learning. This learning paradigm has shown promising results for the challenging problem of anomaly detection, where the general task is to deal with heavy class imbalance. Our paper presents a new approach to few-shot classification, where we employ the knowledge-base of multiple pre-trained convolutional models that act as the backbone for our proposed few-shot framework. Our framework uses a novel ensembling technique for boosting the accuracy while drastically decreasing the total parameter count, thus paving the way for real-time implementation. We perform an extensive hyperparameter search using a power-line defect detection dataset and obtain an accuracy of 92.30% for the 5-way 5-shot task. Without further tuning, we evaluate our model on competing standards with the existing state-of-the-art methods and outperform them.
summary: Over the past few years, there has been a significant improvement in the domain of few-shot learning. This learning paradigm has shown promising results for the challenging problem of anomaly detection, where the general task is to deal with heavy class imbalance. Our paper presents a new approach to few-shot classification, where we employ the knowledge-base of multiple pre-trained convolutional models that act as the backbone for our proposed few-shot framework. Our framework uses a novel ensembling technique for boosting the accuracy while drastically decreasing the total parameter count, thus paving the way for real-time implementation. We perform an extensive hyperparameter search using a power-line defect detection dataset and obtain an accuracy of 92.30% for the 5-way 5-shot task. Without further tuning, we evaluate our model on competing standards with the existing state-of-the-art methods and outperform them.
abstract_zh: 在过去的几年里，小样本学习领域取得了显着的进步。这种学习范式在异常检测这一具有挑战性的问题上显示出了有希望的结果，其中的一般任务是处理严重的类别不平衡。我们的论文提出了一种新的少样本分类方法，其中我们采用多个预先训练的卷积模型的知识库，作为我们提出的少样本框架的支柱。我们的框架使用一种新颖的集成技术来提高准确性，同时大幅减少总参数数量，从而为实时实现铺平道路。我们使用电力线缺陷检测数据集执行广泛的超参数搜索，并获得 5 路 5 次任务的 92.30% 的准确率。无需进一步调整，我们就可以根据现有最先进方法的竞争标准来评估我们的模型，并超越它们。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于卷积集成的少样本缺陷检测方法，通过集成多个预训练模型提升检测准确率并减少参数量。
AI.motivation: 解决工业缺陷检测中样本稀缺与类别不平衡问题，同时实现高效实时检测。
AI.method: 采用多预训练卷积模型作为骨干网络，结合新型集成技术降低参数量并提升准确率。
AI.result: 在电力线缺陷数据集上5-way 5-shot任务达到92.30%准确率，优于现有最先进方法。
AI.conclusion: 所提方法在保持高精度的同时显著减少计算量，适用于实际工业场景的实时缺陷检测需求。

authors_text: Jing Xu,Megan Ung,Mojtaba Komeili,Kushal Arora,Y-Lan Boureau,Jason Weston
title: Learning New Skills after Deployment: Improving open-domain internet-driven dialogue with human feedback
title_zh: 部署后学习新技能：通过人类反馈改进开放域互联网驱动的对话
abstract: Frozen models trained to mimic static datasets can never improve their performance. Models that can employ internet-retrieval for up-to-date information and obtain feedback from humans during deployment provide the promise of both adapting to new information, and improving their performance. In this work we study how to improve internet-driven conversational skills in such a learning framework. We collect deployment data, which we make publicly available, of human interactions, and collect various types of human feedback -- including binary quality measurements, free-form text feedback, and fine-grained reasons for failure. We then study various algorithms for improving from such feedback, including standard supervised learning, rejection sampling, model-guiding and reward-based learning, in order to make recommendations on which type of feedback and algorithms work best. We find the recently introduced Director model (Arora et al., '22) shows significant improvements over other existing approaches.
summary: Frozen models trained to mimic static datasets can never improve their performance. Models that can employ internet-retrieval for up-to-date information and obtain feedback from humans during deployment provide the promise of both adapting to new information, and improving their performance. In this work we study how to improve internet-driven conversational skills in such a learning framework. We collect deployment data, which we make publicly available, of human interactions, and collect various types of human feedback -- including binary quality measurements, free-form text feedback, and fine-grained reasons for failure. We then study various algorithms for improving from such feedback, including standard supervised learning, rejection sampling, model-guiding and reward-based learning, in order to make recommendations on which type of feedback and algorithms work best. We find the recently introduced Director model (Arora et al., '22) shows significant improvements over other existing approaches.
abstract_zh: 经过训练来模仿静态数据集的冻结模型永远无法提高其性能。可以通过互联网检索最新信息并在部署过程中获取人类反馈的模型有望适应新信息并提高其性能。在这项工作中，我们研究如何在这样的学习框架中提高互联网驱动的会话技能。我们收集公开的人类交互的部署数据，并收集各种类型的人类反馈——包括二进制质量测量、自由格式文本反馈和失败的细粒度原因。然后，我们研究各种算法以从此类反馈中进行改进，包括标准监督学习、拒绝采样、模型指导和基于奖励的学习，以便就哪种类型的反馈和算法最有效提出建议。我们发现最近引入的 Director 模型（Arora 等人，'22）比其他现有方法显示出显着的改进。

AI.tldr: 研究如何在部署后通过人类反馈提升开放域互联网驱动对话系统的技能
AI.motivation: 解决现有模型无法持续改进的问题，利用互联网检索和人类反馈实现对话系统性能提升
AI.method: 收集部署数据和多类型人类反馈，对比监督学习、拒绝采样、模型引导和奖励学习等算法，评估不同反馈类型的效果
AI.result: 发现Director模型在多种反馈和算法中表现最佳，显著提升对话性能
AI.conclusion: 推荐使用Director模型结合特定人类反馈机制，以有效提升开放域对话系统的适应性和性能

authors_text: Prathamesh Dharangutte,Christopher Musco
title: A Tight Analysis of Hutchinson's Diagonal Estimator
title_zh: Hutchinson 对角线估计量的严格分析
comment: To appear in SIAM Symposium on Simplicity in Algorithms (SOSA23)
comments: To appear in SIAM Symposium on Simplicity in Algorithms (SOSA23)
abstract: Let $\mathbf{A}\in \mathbb{R}^{n\times n}$ be a matrix with diagonal $\text{diag}(\mathbf{A})$ and let $\bar{\mathbf{A}}$ be $\mathbf{A}$ with its diagonal set to all zeros. We show that Hutchinson's estimator run for $m$ iterations returns a diagonal estimate $\tilde{d}\in \mathbb{R}^n$ such that with probability $(1-δ)$, $$\|\tilde{d} - \text{diag}(\mathbf{A})\|_2 \leq c\sqrt{\frac{\log(2/δ)}{m}}\|\bar{\mathbf{A}}\|_F,$$ where $c$ is a fixed constant independent of all other parameters. This results improves on a recent result of [Baston and Nakatsukasa, 2022] by a $\log(n)$ factor, yielding a bound that is independent of the matrix dimension $n$.
summary: Let $\mathbf{A}\in \mathbb{R}^{n\times n}$ be a matrix with diagonal $\text{diag}(\mathbf{A})$ and let $\bar{\mathbf{A}}$ be $\mathbf{A}$ with its diagonal set to all zeros. We show that Hutchinson's estimator run for $m$ iterations returns a diagonal estimate $\tilde{d}\in \mathbb{R}^n$ such that with probability $(1-δ)$, $$\|\tilde{d} - \text{diag}(\mathbf{A})\|_2 \leq c\sqrt{\frac{\log(2/δ)}{m}}\|\bar{\mathbf{A}}\|_F,$$ where $c$ is a fixed constant independent of all other parameters. This results improves on a recent result of [Baston and Nakatsukasa, 2022] by a $\log(n)$ factor, yielding a bound that is independent of the matrix dimension $n$.
abstract_zh: 令 $\mathbf{A}\in \mathbb{R}^{n\times n}$ 为对角线 $\text{diag}(\mathbf{A})$ 的矩阵，并令 $\bar{\mathbf{A}}$ 为 $\mathbf{A}$，其对角线设置为全零。我们证明 Hutchinson 估计器运行 $m$ 次迭代返回对角线估计 $\tilde{d}\in \mathbb{R}^n$，这样概率为 $(1-δ)$，$$\|\tilde{d} - \text{diag}(\mathbf{A})\|_2 \leq c\sqrt{\frac{\log(2/δ)}{m}}\|\bar{\mathbf{A}}\|_F,$$ 其中 $c$ 是独立于所有其他参数的固定常数。该结果在 [Baston 和 Nakatsukasa，2022] 的最新结果的基础上改进了 $\log(n)$ 因子，产生了一个独立于矩阵维度 $n$ 的界限。

AI.tldr: 本文对Hutchinson对角线估计器进行紧密分析，改进了其误差界。
AI.motivation: 现有结果存在依赖矩阵维度n的log(n)因子，本文旨在消除该因子并获得更优的误差界。
AI.method: 通过分析估计器的随机投影特性，结合矩阵范数分解建立误差上界证明。
AI.result: 证明在m次迭代下，估计误差以高概率满足与log(2/δ)/m相关的上界，且与n无关。
AI.conclusion: 提出了首个与矩阵维度无关的Hutchinson估计器误差界，改进了现有结果的理论保证。

authors_text: Manuel Rebol,Colton Hood,Claudia Ranniger,Adam Rutenberg,Neal Sikka,Erin Maria Horan,Christian Gütl,Krzysztof Pietroszek
title: Remote Assistance with Mixed Reality for Procedural Tasks
title_zh: 针对程序任务的混合现实远程协助
comment: Published in: 2021 IEEE Conference on Virtual Reality and 3D User Interfaces Abstracts and Workshops (VRW)
comments: Published in: 2021 IEEE Conference on Virtual Reality and 3D User Interfaces Abstracts and Workshops (VRW)
abstract: We present a volumetric communication system that is designed for remote assistance of procedural tasks. The system allows a remote expert to visually guide a local operator. The two parties share a view that is spatially identical, but for the local operator it is of the object on which they operate, while for the remote expert, the object is presented as a mixed reality "hologram". Guidance is provided by voice, gestures, and annotations performed directly on the object of interest or its hologram. At each end of the communication, spatial is visualized using mixed-reality glasses.
summary: We present a volumetric communication system that is designed for remote assistance of procedural tasks. The system allows a remote expert to visually guide a local operator. The two parties share a view that is spatially identical, but for the local operator it is of the object on which they operate, while for the remote expert, the object is presented as a mixed reality "hologram". Guidance is provided by voice, gestures, and annotations performed directly on the object of interest or its hologram. At each end of the communication, spatial is visualized using mixed-reality glasses.
abstract_zh: 我们提出了一种体积通信系统，专为程序任务的远程协助而设计。该系统允许远程专家直观地指导本地操作员。双方共享空间上相同的视图，但对于本地操作员来说，这是他们操作的对象的视图，而对于远程专家来说，该对象呈现为混合现实“全息图”。通过直接在感兴趣的物体或其全息图上执行的语音、手势和注释来提供指导。在通信的每一端，使用混合现实眼镜来可视化空间。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于混合现实的远程协助系统，用于指导操作人员完成流程性任务。
AI.motivation: 在需要专家远程指导的场景中（如设备维护或医疗手术），传统通信方式存在信息传递不直观的问题，亟需更高效的可视化协作工具。
AI.method: 通过体感通信系统实现远程专家与本地操作员的空间视角同步，本地端显示真实物体，远程端通过混合现实眼镜观察物体的全息投影，并支持语音、手势及标注交互。
AI.result: 系统验证了混合现实技术可有效提升远程指导的准确性与效率，实现跨地域的实时协同操作。
AI.conclusion: 混合现实技术为远程流程性任务协作提供了沉浸式交互新范式，具有广泛的应用前景。

authors_text: Ziyi Zhao,Sena Kiciroglu,Hugues Vinzant,Yuan Cheng,Isinsu Katircioglu,Mathieu Salzmann,Pascal Fua
title: 3D Pose Based Feedback for Physical Exercises
title_zh: 基于 3D 姿势的体育锻炼反馈
abstract: Unsupervised self-rehabilitation exercises and physical training can cause serious injuries if performed incorrectly. We introduce a learning-based framework that identifies the mistakes made by a user and proposes corrective measures for easier and safer individual training. Our framework does not rely on hard-coded, heuristic rules. Instead, it learns them from data, which facilitates its adaptation to specific user needs. To this end, we use a Graph Convolutional Network (GCN) architecture acting on the user's pose sequence to model the relationship between the body joints trajectories. To evaluate our approach, we introduce a dataset with 3 different physical exercises. Our approach yields 90.9% mistake identification accuracy and successfully corrects 94.2% of the mistakes.
summary: Unsupervised self-rehabilitation exercises and physical training can cause serious injuries if performed incorrectly. We introduce a learning-based framework that identifies the mistakes made by a user and proposes corrective measures for easier and safer individual training. Our framework does not rely on hard-coded, heuristic rules. Instead, it learns them from data, which facilitates its adaptation to specific user needs. To this end, we use a Graph Convolutional Network (GCN) architecture acting on the user's pose sequence to model the relationship between the body joints trajectories. To evaluate our approach, we introduce a dataset with 3 different physical exercises. Our approach yields 90.9% mistake identification accuracy and successfully corrects 94.2% of the mistakes.
abstract_zh: 如果执行不当，无人监督的自我康复练习和体能训练可能会导致严重伤害。我们引入了一个基于学习的框架，可以识别用户所犯的错误并提出纠正措施，以实现更轻松、更安全的个人培训。我们的框架不依赖于硬编码的启发式规则。相反，它从数据中学习它们，这有助于它适应特定的用户需求。为此，我们使用作用于用户姿势序列的图卷积网络（GCN）架构来对身体关节轨迹之间的关系进行建模。为了评估我们的方法，我们引入了包含 3 种不同体育锻炼的数据集。我们的方法产生了 90.9% 的错误识别准确率，并成功纠正了 94.2% 的错误。

search_text: 3D Pose Based Feedback for Physical Exercises 基于 3D 姿势的体育锻炼反馈 Unsupervised self-rehabilitation exercises and physical training can cause serious injuries if performed incorrectly. We introduce a learning-based framework that identifies the mistakes made by a user and proposes corrective measures for easier and safer individual training. Our framework does not rely on hard-coded, heuristic rules. Instead, it learns them from data, which facilitates its adaptation to specific user needs. To this end, we use a Graph Convolutional Network (GCN) architecture acting on the user's pose sequence to model the relationship between the body joints trajectories. To evaluate our approach, we introduce a dataset with 3 different physical exercises. Our approach yields 90.9% mistake identification accuracy and successfully corrects 94.2% of the mistakes. 如果执行不当，无人监督的自我康复练习和体能训练可能会导致严重伤害。我们引入了一个基于学习的框架，可以识别用户所犯的错误并提出纠正措施，以实现更轻松、更安全的个人培训。我们的框架不依赖于硬编码的启发式规则。相反，它从数据中学习它们，这有助于它适应特定的用户需求。为此，我们使用作用于用户姿势序列的图卷积网络（GCN）架构来对身体关节轨迹之间的关系进行建模。为了评估我们的方法，我们引入了包含 3 种不同体育锻炼的数据集。我们的方法产生了 90.9% 的错误识别准确率，并成功纠正了 94.2% 的错误。 本文提出一种基于3D姿态数据的反馈框架，用于识别和纠正物理训练中的错误动作，提升训练安全性。 Ziyi Zhao,Sena Kiciroglu,Hugues Vinzant,Yuan Cheng,Isinsu Katircioglu,Mathieu Salzmann,Pascal Fua cs.CV

authors_text: Manuel Rebol,Christian Gütl,Krzysztof Pietroszek
title: Real-time Gesture Animation Generation from Speech for Virtual Human Interaction
title_zh: 从语音生成实时手势动画以实现虚拟人机交互
comment: CHI EA '21: Extended Abstracts of the 2021 CHI Conference on Human Factors in Computing Systems. arXiv admin note: text overlap with arXiv:2107.00712
comments: CHI EA '21: Extended Abstracts of the 2021 CHI Conference on Human Factors in Computing Systems. arXiv admin note: text overlap with arXiv:2107.00712
abstract: We propose a real-time system for synthesizing gestures directly from speech. Our data-driven approach is based on Generative Adversarial Neural Networks to model the speech-gesture relationship. We utilize the large amount of speaker video data available online to train our 3D gesture model. Our model generates speaker-specific gestures by taking consecutive audio input chunks of two seconds in length. We animate the predicted gestures on a virtual avatar. We achieve a delay below three seconds between the time of audio input and gesture animation. Code and videos are available at https://github.com/mrebol/Gestures-From-Speech
summary: We propose a real-time system for synthesizing gestures directly from speech. Our data-driven approach is based on Generative Adversarial Neural Networks to model the speech-gesture relationship. We utilize the large amount of speaker video data available online to train our 3D gesture model. Our model generates speaker-specific gestures by taking consecutive audio input chunks of two seconds in length. We animate the predicted gestures on a virtual avatar. We achieve a delay below three seconds between the time of audio input and gesture animation. Code and videos are available at https://github.com/mrebol/Gestures-From-Speech
abstract_zh: 我们提出了一种直接从语音合成手势的实时系统。我们的数据驱动方法基于生成对抗神经网络来建模语音手势关系。我们利用在线提供的大量演讲者视频数据来训练我们的 3D 手势模型。我们的模型通过获取两秒长度的连续音频输入块来生成特定于说话者的手势。我们在虚拟化身上制作预测手势的动画。我们在音频输入和手势动画之间实现了低于三秒的延迟。代码和视频可在 https://github.com/mrebol/Gestures-From-Speech 获取

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种从语音实时生成手势动画的系统，用于虚拟人类交互。
AI.motivation: 为了增强虚拟人类与用户之间的自然交互，需要将语音输入实时转化为对应的手势动画。
AI.method: 基于生成对抗神经网络（GAN），利用网络上的大量说话者视频数据训练3D手势模型，通过连续两秒的音频输入生成特定说话者的手势并驱动虚拟人偶。
AI.result: 实现了音频输入到手势动画的延迟低于三秒，代码和视频已开源。
AI.conclusion: 该方法有效实现了语音到手势的实时生成，为虚拟人类交互提供了新的技术手段。

authors: Wil M. P. van der Aalst
authors_json: Wil M. P. van der Aalst
authors_text: Jan Niklas Adams,Daniel Schuster,Seth Schmitz,Günther Schuh,Wil M. P. van der Aalst
title: Defining Cases and Variants for Object-Centric Event Data
title_zh: 定义以对象为中心的事件数据的案例和变体
abstract: The execution of processes leaves traces of event data in information systems. These event data can be analyzed through process mining techniques. For traditional process mining techniques, one has to associate each event with exactly one object, e.g., the company's customer. Events related to one object form an event sequence called a case. A case describes an end-to-end run through a process. The cases contained in event data can be used to discover a process model, detect frequent bottlenecks, or learn predictive models. However, events encountered in real-life information systems, e.g., ERP systems, can often be associated with multiple objects. The traditional sequential case concept falls short of these object-centric event data as these data exhibit a graph structure. One might force object-centric event data into the traditional case concept by flattening it. However, flattening manipulates the data and removes information. Therefore, a concept analogous to the case concept of traditional event logs is necessary to enable the application of different process mining tasks on object-centric event data. In this paper, we introduce the case concept for object-centric process mining: process executions. These are graph-based generalizations of cases as considered in traditional process mining. Furthermore, we provide techniques to extract process executions. Based on these executions, we determine equivalent process behavior with respect to an attribute using graph isomorphism. Equivalent process executions with respect to the event's activity are object-centric variants, i.e., a generalization of variants in traditional process mining. We provide a visualization technique for object-centric variants. The contribution's scalability and efficiency are extensively evaluated. Furthermore, we provide a case study showing the most frequent object-centric variants of a real-life event log.

For traditional process mining techniques, one has to associate each event with exactly one object, e.g., the company's customer. Events related to one object form an event sequence called a case. A case describes an end-to-end run through a process. The cases contained in event data can be used to discover a process model, detect frequent bottlenecks, or learn predictive models. However, events encountered in real-life information systems, e.g., ERP systems, can often be associated with multiple objects. The traditional sequential case concept falls short of these object-centric event data as these data exhibit a graph structure. One might force object-centric event data into the traditional case concept by flattening it. However, flattening manipulates the data and removes information. Therefore, a concept analogous to the case concept of traditional event logs is necessary to enable the application of different process mining tasks on object-centric event data. In this paper, we introduce the case concept for object-centric process mining: process executions. These are graph-based generalizations of cases as considered in traditional process mining. Furthermore, we provide techniques to extract process executions. Based on these executions, we determine equivalent process behavior with respect to an attribute using graph isomorphism. Equivalent process executions with respect to the event's activity are object-centric variants, i.e., a generalization of variants in traditional process mining. We provide a visualization technique for object-centric variants. The contribution's scalability and efficiency are extensively evaluated. Furthermore, we provide a case study showing the most frequent object-centric variants of a real-life event log.
abstract_zh: 流程的执行会在信息系统中留下事件数据的痕迹。这些事件数据可以通过流程挖掘技术进行分析。对于传统的流程​​挖掘技术，必须将每个事件与一个对象（例如公司的客户）相关联。与一个对象相关的事件形成称为事件的事件序列。案例描述了流程的端到端运行。事件数据中包含的案例可用于发现流程模型、检测常见瓶颈或学习预测模型。然而，现实生活中的信息系统（例如 ERP 系统）中遇到的事件通常可以与多个对象相关联。传统的顺序案例概念无法满足这些以对象为中心的事件数据，因为这些数据呈现出图形结构。人们可能会通过扁平化的方式将以对象为中心的事件数据强行纳入传统的案例概念中。然而，扁平化会操纵数据并删除信息。因此，需要一个类似于传统事件日志的案例概念的概念，以便能够在以对象为中心的事件数据上应用不同的流程挖掘任务。在本文中，我们介绍了以对象为中心的流程挖掘的案例概念：流程执行。这些是传统流程挖掘中考虑的基于图形的案例概括。此外，我们还提供提取流程执行的技术。基于这些执行，我们使用图同构确定与属性相关的等效流程行为。与事件活动相关的等效流程执行是以对象为中心的变体，即传统流程挖掘中变体的概括。我们为以对象为中心的变体提供可视化技术。该贡献的可扩展性和效率得到了广泛的评估。此外，我们提供了一个案例研究，显示现实生活中事件日志最常见的以对象为中心的变体。

AI.tldr: 本文提出针对面向对象的事件数据的案例和变体定义，以解决传统流程挖掘方法在处理多对象关联事件时的不足。
AI.motivation: 传统流程挖掘要求每个事件仅关联一个对象，但实际系统中事件常涉及多个对象，导致数据呈现图结构，传统方法强制扁平化处理会丢失信息。
AI.method: 引入基于图结构的‘流程执行’概念，通过图同构技术提取变体，并提供可视化方法与可扩展性评估。
AI.result: 提出面向对象的案例定义与变体检测技术，通过案例研究验证方法在真实事件日志中的有效性与效率。
AI.conclusion: 该方法为面向对象的流程挖掘任务提供了理论基础与实用工具，有效支持复杂事件数据的分析需求。

We provide a visualization technique for object-centric variants. The contribution's scalability and efficiency are extensively evaluated. Furthermore, we provide a case study showing the most frequent object-centric variants of a real-life event log. 流程的执行会在信息系统中留下事件数据的痕迹。这些事件数据可以通过流程挖掘技术进行分析。对于传统的流程​​挖掘技术，必须将每个事件与一个对象（例如公司的客户）相关联。与一个对象相关的事件形成称为事件的事件序列。案例描述了流程的端到端运行。事件数据中包含的案例可用于发现流程模型、检测常见瓶颈或学习预测模型。然而，现实生活中的信息系统（例如 ERP 系统）中遇到的事件通常可以与多个对象相关联。传统的顺序案例概念无法满足这些以对象为中心的事件数据，因为这些数据呈现出图形结构。人们可能会通过扁平化的方式将以对象为中心的事件数据强行纳入传统的案例概念中。然而，扁平化会操纵数据并删除信息。因此，需要一个类似于传统事件日志的案例概念的概念，以便能够在以对象为中心的事件数据上应用不同的流程挖掘任务。在本文中，我们介绍了以对象为中心的流程挖掘的案例概念：流程执行。这些是传统流程挖掘中考虑的基于图形的案例概括。此外，我们还提供提取流程执行的技术。基于这些执行，我们使用图同构确定与属性相关的等效流程行为。与事件活动相关的等效流程执行是以对象为中心的变体，即传统流程挖掘中变体的概括。我们为以对象为中心的变体提供可视化技术。该贡献的可扩展性和效率得到了广泛的评估。此外，我们提供了一个案例研究，显示现实生活中事件日志最常见的以对象为中心的变体。 本文提出针对面向对象的事件数据的案例和变体定义，以解决传统流程挖掘方法在处理多对象关联事件时的不足。 Jan Niklas Adams,Daniel Schuster,Seth Schmitz,Günther Schuh,Wil M. P. van der Aalst cs.DB,cs.AI

authors_text: Samuel Joutard,Reuben Dorent,Sebastien Ourselin,Tom Vercauteren,Marc Modat
title: Driving Points Prediction For Abdominal Probabilistic Registration
title_zh: 腹部概率配准的驱动点预测
abstract: Inter-patient abdominal registration has various applications, from pharmakinematic studies to anatomy modeling. Yet, it remains a challenging application due to the morphological heterogeneity and variability of the human abdomen. Among the various registration methods proposed for this task, probabilistic displacement registration models estimate displacement distribution for a subset of points by comparing feature vectors of points from the two images. These probabilistic models are informative and robust while allowing large displacements by design. As the displacement distributions are typically estimated on a subset of points (which we refer to as driving points), due to computational requirements, we propose in this work to learn a driving points predictor. Compared to previously proposed methods, the driving points predictor is optimized in an end-to-end fashion to infer driving points tailored for a specific registration pipeline. We evaluate the impact of our contribution on two different datasets corresponding to different modalities. Specifically, we compared the performances of 6 different probabilistic displacement registration models when using a driving points predictor or one of 2 other standard driving points selection methods. The proposed method improved performances in 11 out of 12 experiments.
summary: Inter-patient abdominal registration has various applications, from pharmakinematic studies to anatomy modeling. Yet, it remains a challenging application due to the morphological heterogeneity and variability of the human abdomen. Among the various registration methods proposed for this task, probabilistic displacement registration models estimate displacement distribution for a subset of points by comparing feature vectors of points from the two images. These probabilistic models are informative and robust while allowing large displacements by design. As the displacement distributions are typically estimated on a subset of points (which we refer to as driving points), due to computational requirements, we propose in this work to learn a driving points predictor. Compared to previously proposed methods, the driving points predictor is optimized in an end-to-end fashion to infer driving points tailored for a specific registration pipeline. We evaluate the impact of our contribution on two different datasets corresponding to different modalities. Specifically, we compared the performances of 6 different probabilistic displacement registration models when using a driving points predictor or one of 2 other standard driving points selection methods. The proposed method improved performances in 11 out of 12 experiments.
abstract_zh: 患者间腹部登记有多种应用，从药代动力学研究到解剖建模。然而，由于人类腹部的形态异质性和变异性，它仍然是一个具有挑战性的应用。在为此任务提出的各种配准方法中，概率位移配准模型通过比较两个图像中的点的特征向量来估计点子集的位移分布。这些概率模型信息丰富且稳健，同时允许设计大位移。由于位移分布通常是在点的子集（我们称为驱动点）上估计的，由于计算要求，我们建议在这项工作中学习驱动点预测器。与之前提出的方法相比，驱动点预测器以端到端的方式进行优化，以推断为特定注册管道定制的驱动点。我们评估我们的贡献对对应于不同模式的两个不同数据集的影响。具体来说，我们比较了使用驱动点预测器或其他 2 种标准驱动点选择方法之一时 6 种不同的概率位移配准模型的性能。该方法在 12 次实验中的 11 次中提高了性能。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种端到端的驱动点预测器，用于优化腹部概率配准中的驱动点选择，以提升配准效果。
AI.motivation: 传统方法在驱动点选择上缺乏针对性，导致腹部配准因形态多样性而效果受限，需更高效的驱动点预测策略。
AI.method: 设计并训练一个深度学习模型作为驱动点预测器，通过端到端优化生成适配特定配准流程的驱动点集。
AI.result: 在两个不同模态的数据集上，新方法在12个实验中有11个实验中优于两种传统驱动点选择方法。
AI.conclusion: 驱动点预测器能有效提升概率配准性能，验证了端到端优化策略在医学图像配准中的优势。

authors_text: Keegan Quigley,Miriam Cha,Ruizhi Liao,Geeticka Chauhan,Steven Horng,Seth Berkowitz,Polina Golland
title: RadTex: Learning Efficient Radiograph Representations from Text Reports
title_zh: RadTex：从文本报告中学习有效的射线照相表示
comment: Awarded Best Paper at Resource Efficient Medical Image Analysis (REMIA) Workshop, MICCAI 2022
comments: Awarded Best Paper at Resource Efficient Medical Image Analysis (REMIA) Workshop, MICCAI 2022
abstract: Automated analysis of chest radiography using deep learning has tremendous potential to enhance the clinical diagnosis of diseases in patients. However, deep learning models typically require large amounts of annotated data to achieve high performance -- often an obstacle to medical domain adaptation. In this paper, we build a data-efficient learning framework that utilizes radiology reports to improve medical image classification performance with limited labeled data (fewer than 1000 examples). Specifically, we examine image-captioning pretraining to learn high-quality medical image representations that train on fewer examples. Following joint pretraining of a convolutional encoder and transformer decoder, we transfer the learned encoder to various classification tasks. Averaged over 9 pathologies, we find that our model achieves higher classification performance than ImageNet-supervised and in-domain supervised pretraining when labeled training data is limited.
summary: Automated analysis of chest radiography using deep learning has tremendous potential to enhance the clinical diagnosis of diseases in patients. However, deep learning models typically require large amounts of annotated data to achieve high performance -- often an obstacle to medical domain adaptation. In this paper, we build a data-efficient learning framework that utilizes radiology reports to improve medical image classification performance with limited labeled data (fewer than 1000 examples). Specifically, we examine image-captioning pretraining to learn high-quality medical image representations that train on fewer examples. Following joint pretraining of a convolutional encoder and transformer decoder, we transfer the learned encoder to various classification tasks. Averaged over 9 pathologies, we find that our model achieves higher classification performance than ImageNet-supervised and in-domain supervised pretraining when labeled training data is limited.
abstract_zh: 使用深度学习对胸部放射线照片进行自动分析在增强患者疾病的临床诊断方面具有巨大的潜力。然而，深度学习模型通常需要大量带注释的数据才能实现高性能——这通常是医学领域适应的障碍。在本文中，我们构建了一个数据高效的学习框架，利用放射学报告以有限的标记数据（少于 1000 个示例）提高医学图像分类性能。具体来说，我们检查图像字幕预训练，以学习在较少示例上进行训练的高质量医学图像表示。在对卷积编码器和转换器解码器进行联合预训练之后，我们将学习到的编码器转移到各种分类任务中。对 9 种病理学进行平均，我们发现当标记训练数据有限时，我们的模型比 ImageNet 监督和域内监督预训练实现了更高的分类性能。

AI.tldr: 本文提出RadTex框架，通过利用放射科报告中的文本信息，在有限标注数据下提升医学影像分类性能。
AI.motivation: 医学影像分析需大量标注数据，而实际医疗场景中数据获取困难，限制了深度学习模型的适应性。
AI.method: 采用图像-文本预训练方法，联合训练卷积编码器与Transformer解码器，通过迁移学习将编码器应用于多种分类任务。
AI.result: 在9种病理分类任务中，当标注数据少于1000例时，模型性能优于ImageNet监督预训练和领域内监督预训练方法。
AI.conclusion: 基于文本报告的预训练能有效提升医学影像分类在数据稀缺场景下的表现，为医疗AI提供高效解决方案。

authors_text: Camila Gonzalez,Karol Gotkowski,Moritz Fuchs,Andreas Bucher,Armin Dadras,Ricarda Fischbach,Isabel Kaltenborn,Anirban Mukhopadhyay
title: Distance-based detection of out-of-distribution silent failures for Covid-19 lung lesion segmentation
title_zh: 基于距离的 Covid-19 肺部病灶分割分布外静默故障检测
abstract: Automatic segmentation of ground glass opacities and consolidations in chest computer tomography (CT) scans can potentially ease the burden of radiologists during times of high resource utilisation. However, deep learning models are not trusted in the clinical routine due to failing silently on out-of-distribution (OOD) data. We propose a lightweight OOD detection method that leverages the Mahalanobis distance in the feature space and seamlessly integrates into state-of-the-art segmentation pipelines. The simple approach can even augment pre-trained models with clinically relevant uncertainty quantification. We validate our method across four chest CT distribution shifts and two magnetic resonance imaging applications, namely segmentation of the hippocampus and the prostate. Our results show that the proposed method effectively detects far- and near-OOD samples across all explored scenarios.
summary: Automatic segmentation of ground glass opacities and consolidations in chest computer tomography (CT) scans can potentially ease the burden of radiologists during times of high resource utilisation. However, deep learning models are not trusted in the clinical routine due to failing silently on out-of-distribution (OOD) data. We propose a lightweight OOD detection method that leverages the Mahalanobis distance in the feature space and seamlessly integrates into state-of-the-art segmentation pipelines. The simple approach can even augment pre-trained models with clinically relevant uncertainty quantification. We validate our method across four chest CT distribution shifts and two magnetic resonance imaging applications, namely segmentation of the hippocampus and the prostate. Our results show that the proposed method effectively detects far- and near-OOD samples across all explored scenarios.
abstract_zh: 在胸部计算机断层扫描 (CT) 扫描中自动分割毛玻璃混浊和实变可以潜在地减轻放射科医生在资源利用率高的时期的负担。然而，由于深度学习模型在分布外（OOD）数据上默默地失败，因此在临床常规中并不被信任。我们提出了一种轻量级 OOD 检测方法，该方法利用特征空间中的 Mahalanobis 距离并无缝集成到最先进的分割流程中。这种简单的方法甚至可以通过临床相关的不确定性量化来增强预先训练的模型。我们在四个胸部 CT 分布变化和两个磁共振成像应用（即海马体和前列腺的分割）中验证了我们的方法。我们的结果表明，所提出的方法可以有效地检测所有探索场景中的远距和近距 OOD 样本。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于马氏距离的轻量级方法，用于检测新冠肺部病变分割模型中的分布外静默故障。
AI.motivation: 深度学习模型在临床环境中因对分布外数据静默失效而缺乏信任，需开发可靠方法检测此类故障以提高安全性。
AI.method: 利用特征空间的马氏距离构建OOD检测模块，无缝集成至分割流程，并增强预训练模型的不确定性量化能力。
AI.result: 方法在四个胸部CT分布偏移和两个MRI任务中均有效检测远近OOD样本，验证了其广泛适用性。
AI.conclusion: 提出的方法以轻量且有效的方式解决分割模型的分布外故障检测问题，提升了临床应用的可靠性。

search_text: Distance-based detection of out-of-distribution silent failures for Covid-19 lung lesion segmentation 基于距离的 Covid-19 肺部病灶分割分布外静默故障检测 Automatic segmentation of ground glass opacities and consolidations in chest computer tomography (CT) scans can potentially ease the burden of radiologists during times of high resource utilisation. However, deep learning models are not trusted in the clinical routine due to failing silently on out-of-distribution (OOD) data. We propose a lightweight OOD detection method that leverages the Mahalanobis distance in the feature space and seamlessly integrates into state-of-the-art segmentation pipelines. The simple approach can even augment pre-trained models with clinically relevant uncertainty quantification. We validate our method across four chest CT distribution shifts and two magnetic resonance imaging applications, namely segmentation of the hippocampus and the prostate. Our results show that the proposed method effectively detects far- and near-OOD samples across all explored scenarios. 在胸部计算机断层扫描 (CT) 扫描中自动分割毛玻璃混浊和实变可以潜在地减轻放射科医生在资源利用率高的时期的负担。然而，由于深度学习模型在分布外（OOD）数据上默默地失败，因此在临床常规中并不被信任。我们提出了一种轻量级 OOD 检测方法，该方法利用特征空间中的 Mahalanobis 距离并无缝集成到最先进的分割流程中。这种简单的方法甚至可以通过临床相关的不确定性量化来增强预先训练的模型。我们在四个胸部 CT 分布变化和两个磁共振成像应用（即海马体和前列腺的分割）中验证了我们的方法。我们的结果表明，所提出的方法可以有效地检测所有探索场景中的远距和近距 OOD 样本。 本文提出一种基于马氏距离的轻量级方法，用于检测新冠肺部病变分割模型中的分布外静默故障。 Camila Gonzalez,Karol Gotkowski,Moritz Fuchs,Andreas Bucher,Armin Dadras,Ricarda Fischbach,Isabel Kaltenborn,Anirban Mukhopadhyay eess.IV,cs.CV,cs.LG

authors_text: Qingyang Wang,Michael A. Powell,Ali Geisa,Eric Bridgeford,Carey E. Priebe,Joshua T. Vogelstein
title: Why do networks have inhibitory/negative connections?
title_zh: 为什么网络具有抑制/负向连接？
abstract: Why do brains have inhibitory connections? Why do deep networks have negative weights? We propose an answer from the perspective of representation capacity. We believe representing functions is the primary role of both (i) the brain in natural intelligence, and (ii) deep networks in artificial intelligence. Our answer to why there are inhibitory/negative weights is: to learn more functions. We prove that, in the absence of negative weights, neural networks with non-decreasing activation functions are not universal approximators. While this may be an intuitive result to some, to the best of our knowledge, there is no formal theory, in either machine learning or neuroscience, that demonstrates why negative weights are crucial in the context of representation capacity. Further, we provide insights on the geometric properties of the representation space that non-negative deep networks cannot represent. We expect these insights will yield a deeper understanding of more sophisticated inductive priors imposed on the distribution of weights that lead to more efficient biological and machine learning.
summary: Why do brains have inhibitory connections? Why do deep networks have negative weights? We propose an answer from the perspective of representation capacity. We believe representing functions is the primary role of both (i) the brain in natural intelligence, and (ii) deep networks in artificial intelligence. Our answer to why there are inhibitory/negative weights is: to learn more functions. We prove that, in the absence of negative weights, neural networks with non-decreasing activation functions are not universal approximators. While this may be an intuitive result to some, to the best of our knowledge, there is no formal theory, in either machine learning or neuroscience, that demonstrates why negative weights are crucial in the context of representation capacity. Further, we provide insights on the geometric properties of the representation space that non-negative deep networks cannot represent. We expect these insights will yield a deeper understanding of more sophisticated inductive priors imposed on the distribution of weights that lead to more efficient biological and machine learning.
abstract_zh: 为什么大脑有抑制性连接？为什么深度网络有负权重？我们从代表性能力的角度提出答案。我们认为表示函数是（i）自然智能中的大脑和（ii）人工智能中的深层网络的主要角色。我们对为什么存在抑制/负权重的答案是：学习更多函数。我们证明，在没有负权重的情况下，具有非递减激活函数的神经网络不是通用逼近器。虽然这对某些人来说可能是直观的结果，但据我们所知，无论是机器学习还是神经科学，都没有正式的理论可以证明为什么负权重在表示能力的背景下至关重要。此外，我们还提供了关于非负深度网络无法表示的表示空间的几何特性的见解。我们期望这些见解将使人们对权重分布上施加的更复杂的归纳先验产生更深入的理解，从而实现更高效的生物和机器学习。

AI.tldr: 本文探讨了为什么网络中存在抑制性/负连接，从表示能力的角度进行分析。
AI.motivation: 本文旨在解释为什么大脑和深度网络中存在抑制性连接或负权重，认为其主要作用是增强表示能力，从而学习更多函数。
AI.method: 通过理论证明，没有负权重的神经网络在使用非递减激活函数时无法成为通用逼近器，并分析了非负深度网络无法表示的表示空间的几何特性。
AI.result: 证明了负权重对于神经网络的表示能力至关重要，并揭示了非负深度网络在表示空间上的局限性。
AI.conclusion: 负权重在表示能力方面具有重要作用，有助于更高效的生物和机器学习，未来研究可进一步探索权重分布的归纳先验。

authors_text: Sophie Noiret,Jennifer Lumetzberger,Martin Kampel
title: Bias and Fairness in Computer Vision Applications of the Criminal Justice System
title_zh: 刑事司法系统计算机视觉应用中的偏见和公平
comment: \c{opyright} 2021 IEEE. Personal use of this material is permitted. Permission from IEEE must be obtained for all other uses, in any current or future media, including reprinting/republishing this material for advertising or promotional purposes, creating new collective works, for resale or redistribution to servers or lists, or reuse of any copyrighted component of this work in other works
abstract: Discriminatory practices involving AI-driven police work have been the subject of much controversies in the past few years, with algorithms such as COMPAS, PredPol and ShotSpotter being accused of unfairly impacting minority groups. At the same time, the issues of fairness in machine learning, and in particular in computer vision, have been the subject of a growing number of academic works. In this paper, we examine how these area intersect. We provide information on how these practices have come to exist and the difficulties in alleviating them. We then examine three applications currently in development to understand what risks they pose to fairness and how those risks can be mitigated.
summary: Discriminatory practices involving AI-driven police work have been the subject of much controversies in the past few years, with algorithms such as COMPAS, PredPol and ShotSpotter being accused of unfairly impacting minority groups. At the same time, the issues of fairness in machine learning, and in particular in computer vision, have been the subject of a growing number of academic works. In this paper, we examine how these area intersect. We provide information on how these practices have come to exist and the difficulties in alleviating them. We then examine three applications currently in development to understand what risks they pose to fairness and how those risks can be mitigated.
abstract_zh: 过去几年，人工智能驱动的警察工作中的歧视性做法一直备受争议，COMPAS、PredPol 和 ShotSpotter 等算法被指控不公平地影响少数群体。与此同时，机器学习（尤其是计算机视觉）的公平问题已成为越来越多学术著作的主题。在本文中，我们研究这些区域如何相交。我们提供有关这些做法如何存在以及减轻这些做法的困难的信息。然后，我们检查当前正在开发的三个应用程序，以了解它们对公平性造成哪些风险以及如何减轻这些风险。

AI.tldr: 探讨计算机视觉在刑事司法系统中的偏见与公平性问题，分析其成因、缓解困难，并评估三个应用的风险及应对策略。
AI.motivation: 回应AI警务引发的公平性争议，结合学术研究与实际应用，寻找减少算法歧视的解决方案。
AI.method: 通过文献综述分析算法偏见根源，结合案例研究评估三个刑事司法计算机视觉应用的风险与缓解措施。
AI.result: 揭示了算法歧视的结构性成因，指出技术改进与政策调整的双重挑战，并提出针对性风险缓解建议。
AI.conclusion: 需跨学科协作推动技术伦理框架，平衡算法效率与社会公平，避免AI加剧刑事司法系统中的群体不公。

authors_text: Jichang Li,Guanbin Li,Feng Liu,Yizhou Yu
title: Neighborhood Collective Estimation for Noisy Label Identification and Correction
title_zh: 用于噪声标签识别和纠正的邻域集体估计
comment: Accepted to European Conference on Computer Vision (ECCV), 2022
comments: Accepted to European Conference on Computer Vision (ECCV), 2022
abstract: Learning with noisy labels (LNL) aims at designing strategies to improve model performance and generalization by mitigating the effects of model overfitting to noisy labels. The key success of LNL lies in identifying as many clean samples as possible from massive noisy data, while rectifying the wrongly assigned noisy labels. Recent advances employ the predicted label distributions of individual samples to perform noise verification and noisy label correction, easily giving rise to confirmation bias. To mitigate this issue, we propose Neighborhood Collective Estimation, in which the predictive reliability of a candidate sample is re-estimated by contrasting it against its feature-space nearest neighbors. Specifically, our method is divided into two steps: 1) Neighborhood Collective Noise Verification to separate all training samples into a clean or noisy subset, 2) Neighborhood Collective Label Correction to relabel noisy samples, and then auxiliary techniques are used to assist further model optimization. Extensive experiments on four commonly used benchmark datasets, i.e., CIFAR-10, CIFAR-100, Clothing-1M and Webvision-1.0, demonstrate that our proposed method considerably outperforms state-of-the-art methods.
summary: Learning with noisy labels (LNL) aims at designing strategies to improve model performance and generalization by mitigating the effects of model overfitting to noisy labels. The key success of LNL lies in identifying as many clean samples as possible from massive noisy data, while rectifying the wrongly assigned noisy labels. Recent advances employ the predicted label distributions of individual samples to perform noise verification and noisy label correction, easily giving rise to confirmation bias. To mitigate this issue, we propose Neighborhood Collective Estimation, in which the predictive reliability of a candidate sample is re-estimated by contrasting it against its feature-space nearest neighbors. Specifically, our method is divided into two steps: 1) Neighborhood Collective Noise Verification to separate all training samples into a clean or noisy subset, 2) Neighborhood Collective Label Correction to relabel noisy samples, and then auxiliary techniques are used to assist further model optimization. Extensive experiments on four commonly used benchmark datasets, i.e., CIFAR-10, CIFAR-100, Clothing-1M and Webvision-1.0, demonstrate that our proposed method considerably outperforms state-of-the-art methods.
abstract_zh: 使用噪声标签学习（LNL）旨在设计策略，通过减轻模型对噪声标签过度拟合的影响来提高模型性能和泛化能力。 LNL 的关键成功在于从大量噪声数据中识别出尽可能多的干净样本，同时纠正错误分配的噪声标签。最近的进展利用单个样本的预测标签分布来进行噪声验证和噪声标签校正，很容易引起确认偏差。为了缓解这个问题，我们提出了邻域集体估计，其中通过将候选样本与其特征空间最近邻进行对比来重新估计候选样本的预测可靠性。具体来说，我们的方法分为两个步骤：1）邻域集体噪声验证，将所有训练样本分成干净或噪声子集，2）邻域集体标签校正，重新标记噪声样本，然后使用辅助技术来辅助进一步的模型优化。对四个常用基准数据集（即 CIFAR-10、CIFAR-100、Clothing-1M 和 Webvision-1.0）的广泛实验表明，我们提出的方法大大优于最先进的方法。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种名为Neighborhood Collective Estimation的方法，用于识别和校正噪声标签，以提升带噪声标签的学习模型性能。
AI.motivation: 现有方法通过单一样本的预测分布验证噪声标签，易产生确认偏误。本文旨在通过对比样本与其特征空间近邻的预测可靠性，缓解这一问题。
AI.method: 方法分为两步：1) 邻域集体噪声验证，将样本划分为干净或噪声子集；2) 邻域集体标签校正，重新标注噪声样本，并结合辅助技术优化模型。
AI.result: 在CIFAR-10、CIFAR-100、Clothing-1M和Webvision-1.0四个基准数据集上，该方法显著优于现有最先进方法。
AI.conclusion: 通过利用邻域信息校正噪声标签，本文方法有效降低了确认偏误，提升了模型在噪声标签环境下的准确性。

search_text: Neighborhood Collective Estimation for Noisy Label Identification and Correction 用于噪声标签识别和纠正的邻域集体估计 Learning with noisy labels (LNL) aims at designing strategies to improve model performance and generalization by mitigating the effects of model overfitting to noisy labels. The key success of LNL lies in identifying as many clean samples as possible from massive noisy data, while rectifying the wrongly assigned noisy labels. Recent advances employ the predicted label distributions of individual samples to perform noise verification and noisy label correction, easily giving rise to confirmation bias. To mitigate this issue, we propose Neighborhood Collective Estimation, in which the predictive reliability of a candidate sample is re-estimated by contrasting it against its feature-space nearest neighbors. Specifically, our method is divided into two steps: 1) Neighborhood Collective Noise Verification to separate all training samples into a clean or noisy subset, 2) Neighborhood Collective Label Correction to relabel noisy samples, and then auxiliary techniques are used to assist further model optimization. Extensive experiments on four commonly used benchmark datasets, i.e., CIFAR-10, CIFAR-100, Clothing-1M and Webvision-1.0, demonstrate that our proposed method considerably outperforms state-of-the-art methods. 使用噪声标签学习（LNL）旨在设计策略，通过减轻模型对噪声标签过度拟合的影响来提高模型性能和泛化能力。 LNL 的关键成功在于从大量噪声数据中识别出尽可能多的干净样本，同时纠正错误分配的噪声标签。最近的进展利用单个样本的预测标签分布来进行噪声验证和噪声标签校正，很容易引起确认偏差。为了缓解这个问题，我们提出了邻域集体估计，其中通过将候选样本与其特征空间最近邻进行对比来重新估计候选样本的预测可靠性。具体来说，我们的方法分为两个步骤：1）邻域集体噪声验证，将所有训练样本分成干净或噪声子集，2）邻域集体标签校正，重新标记噪声样本，然后使用辅助技术来辅助进一步的模型优化。对四个常用基准数据集（即 CIFAR-10、CIFAR-100、Clothing-1M 和 Webvision-1.0）的广泛实验表明，我们提出的方法大大优于最先进的方法。 本文提出一种名为Neighborhood Collective Estimation的方法，用于识别和校正噪声标签，以提升带噪声标签的学习模型性能。 Jichang Li,Guanbin Li,Feng Liu,Yizhou Yu cs.CV

authors_text: Camila Gonzalez,Amin Ranem,Ahmed Othman,Anirban Mukhopadhyay
title: Task-agnostic Continual Hippocampus Segmentation for Smooth Population Shifts
title_zh: 与任务无关的连续海马分割，以实现平稳的人口转移
abstract: Most continual learning methods are validated in settings where task boundaries are clearly defined and task identity information is available during training and testing. We explore how such methods perform in a task-agnostic setting that more closely resembles dynamic clinical environments with gradual population shifts. We propose ODEx, a holistic solution that combines out-of-distribution detection with continual learning techniques. Validation on two scenarios of hippocampus segmentation shows that our proposed method reliably maintains performance on earlier tasks without losing plasticity.
summary: Most continual learning methods are validated in settings where task boundaries are clearly defined and task identity information is available during training and testing. We explore how such methods perform in a task-agnostic setting that more closely resembles dynamic clinical environments with gradual population shifts. We propose ODEx, a holistic solution that combines out-of-distribution detection with continual learning techniques. Validation on two scenarios of hippocampus segmentation shows that our proposed method reliably maintains performance on earlier tasks without losing plasticity.
abstract_zh: 大多数持续学习方法在明确定义任务边界并且在训练和测试期间可以获得任务身份信息的环境中得到验证。我们探索这些方法如何在与任务无关的环境中执行，该环境更接近于人口逐渐变化的动态临床环境。我们提出 ODEx，这是一种将分布外检测与持续学习技术相结合的整体解决方案。对海马体分割的两种场景的验证表明，我们提出的方法可靠地保持了早期任务的性能而不失去可塑性。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种任务无关的持续海马体分割方法，以应对动态临床环境中渐进的人口变化。
AI.motivation: 现有持续学习方法依赖明确任务边界，但实际临床场景常面临渐进式人口变化，需探索任务无关的鲁棒解决方案。
AI.method: 提出ODEx方法，结合分布外检测与持续学习技术，实现任务无关的模型更新与性能保持。
AI.result: 在两个海马体分割场景中验证，ODEx能可靠维持早期任务性能且保持模型可塑性。
AI.conclusion: ODEx为动态临床环境下的持续学习提供了有效方案，可应对渐进式人口变化带来的挑战。

authors_text: Jiayu Huo,Vejay Vakharia,Chengyuan Wu,Ashwini Sharan,Andrew Ko,Sebastien Ourselin,Rachel Sparks
title: Brain Lesion Synthesis via Progressive Adversarial Variational Auto-Encoder
title_zh: 通过渐进对抗变分自动编码器进行脑损伤合成
comment: 11 pages, 4 figures, accepted by International Workshop on Simulation and Synthesis in Medical Imaging (SASHIMI 2022)
comments: 11 pages, 4 figures, accepted by International Workshop on Simulation and Synthesis in Medical Imaging (SASHIMI 2022)
abstract: Laser interstitial thermal therapy (LITT) is a novel minimally invasive treatment that is used to ablate intracranial structures to treat mesial temporal lobe epilepsy (MTLE). Region of interest (ROI) segmentation before and after LITT would enable automated lesion quantification to objectively assess treatment efficacy. Deep learning techniques, such as convolutional neural networks (CNNs) are state-of-the-art solutions for ROI segmentation, but require large amounts of annotated data during the training. However, collecting large datasets from emerging treatments such as LITT is impractical. In this paper, we propose a progressive brain lesion synthesis framework (PAVAE) to expand both the quantity and diversity of the training dataset. Concretely, our framework consists of two sequential networks: a mask synthesis network and a mask-guided lesion synthesis network. To better employ extrinsic information to provide additional supervision during network training, we design a condition embedding block (CEB) and a mask embedding block (MEB) to encode inherent conditions of masks to the feature space. Finally, a segmentation network is trained using raw and synthetic lesion images to evaluate the effectiveness of the proposed framework. Experimental results show that our method can achieve realistic synthetic results and boost the performance of down-stream segmentation tasks above traditional data augmentation techniques.
summary: Laser interstitial thermal therapy (LITT) is a novel minimally invasive treatment that is used to ablate intracranial structures to treat mesial temporal lobe epilepsy (MTLE). Region of interest (ROI) segmentation before and after LITT would enable automated lesion quantification to objectively assess treatment efficacy. Deep learning techniques, such as convolutional neural networks (CNNs) are state-of-the-art solutions for ROI segmentation, but require large amounts of annotated data during the training. However, collecting large datasets from emerging treatments such as LITT is impractical. In this paper, we propose a progressive brain lesion synthesis framework (PAVAE) to expand both the quantity and diversity of the training dataset. Concretely, our framework consists of two sequential networks: a mask synthesis network and a mask-guided lesion synthesis network. To better employ extrinsic information to provide additional supervision during network training, we design a condition embedding block (CEB) and a mask embedding block (MEB) to encode inherent conditions of masks to the feature space. Finally, a segmentation network is trained using raw and synthetic lesion images to evaluate the effectiveness of the proposed framework. Experimental results show that our method can achieve realistic synthetic results and boost the performance of down-stream segmentation tasks above traditional data augmentation techniques.
abstract_zh: 激光间质热疗法（LITT）是一种新型微创治疗方法，用于消融颅内结构以治疗内侧颞叶癫痫（MTLE）。 LITT 前后的感兴趣区域 (ROI) 分割可以实现自动病变量化，从而客观评估治疗效果。卷积神经网络 (CNN) 等深度学习技术是最先进的 ROI 分割解决方案，但在训练过程中需要大量带注释的数据。然而，从 LITT 等新兴疗法中收集大型数据集是不切实际的。在本文中，我们提出了一种渐进式脑损伤综合框架（PAVAE）来扩展训练数据集的数量和多样性。具体来说，我们的框架由两个顺序网络组成：掩模合成网络和掩模引导病变合成网络。为了更好地利用外部信息在网络训练期间提供额外的监督，我们设计了一个条件嵌入块（CEB）和一个掩码嵌入块（MEB）来将掩码的固有条件编码到特征空间。最后，使用原始和合成病变图像训练分割网络，以评估所提出框架的有效性。实验结果表明，我们的方法可以实现真实的合成结果，并比传统的数据增强技术提高下游分割任务的性能。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种渐进式对抗变分自编码器（PAVAE）框架，用于合成脑损伤图像以提升ROI分割任务的性能。
AI.motivation: 传统深度学习方法需大量标注数据，而新兴治疗手段如LITT的数据获取困难，限制了模型训练与评估。
AI.method: 设计包含掩码合成网络和掩码引导病变合成网络的双阶段框架，并引入条件嵌入块与掩码嵌入块编码先验信息，结合真实与合成数据训练分割网络。
AI.result: 实验表明该方法生成的合成图像具有高真实性，且分割任务性能优于传统数据增强技术。
AI.conclusion: PAVAE框架有效解决了小样本场景下的数据不足问题，为脑损伤量化评估提供了新思路。

Concretely, our framework consists of two sequential networks: a mask synthesis network and a mask-guided lesion synthesis network. To better employ extrinsic information to provide additional supervision during network training, we design a condition embedding block (CEB) and a mask embedding block (MEB) to encode inherent conditions of masks to the feature space. Finally, a segmentation network is trained using raw and synthetic lesion images to evaluate the effectiveness of the proposed framework. Experimental results show that our method can achieve realistic synthetic results and boost the performance of down-stream segmentation tasks above traditional data augmentation techniques. 激光间质热疗法（LITT）是一种新型微创治疗方法，用于消融颅内结构以治疗内侧颞叶癫痫（MTLE）。 LITT 前后的感兴趣区域 (ROI) 分割可以实现自动病变量化，从而客观评估治疗效果。卷积神经网络 (CNN) 等深度学习技术是最先进的 ROI 分割解决方案，但在训练过程中需要大量带注释的数据。然而，从 LITT 等新兴疗法中收集大型数据集是不切实际的。在本文中，我们提出了一种渐进式脑损伤综合框架（PAVAE）来扩展训练数据集的数量和多样性。具体来说，我们的框架由两个顺序网络组成：掩模合成网络和掩模引导病变合成网络。为了更好地利用外部信息在网络训练期间提供额外的监督，我们设计了一个条件嵌入块（CEB）和一个掩码嵌入块（MEB）来将掩码的固有条件编码到特征空间。最后，使用原始和合成病变图像训练分割网络，以评估所提出框架的有效性。实验结果表明，我们的方法可以实现真实的合成结果，并比传统的数据增强技术提高下游分割任务的性能。 本文提出一种渐进式对抗变分自编码器（PAVAE）框架，用于合成脑损伤图像以提升ROI分割任务的性能。 Jiayu Huo,Vejay Vakharia,Chengyuan Wu,Ashwini Sharan,Andrew Ko,Sebastien Ourselin,Rachel Sparks cs.CV,eess.IV

authors_text: Vinod Kumar Chauhan,Anshul Thakur,Odhran O'Donoghue,David A. Clifton
title: COPER: Continuous Patient State Perceiver
title_zh: 铜：连续患者状态感知器
comment: 2 figures; presented in IEEE International Conference on Biomedical and Health Informatics (IEEE BHI-2022)
comments: 2 figures; presented in IEEE International Conference on Biomedical and Health Informatics (IEEE BHI-2022)
abstract: In electronic health records (EHRs), irregular time-series (ITS) occur naturally due to patient health dynamics, reflected by irregular hospital visits, diseases/conditions and the necessity to measure different vitals signs at each visit etc. ITS present challenges in training machine learning algorithms which mostly are built on assumption of coherent fixed dimensional feature space. In this paper, we propose a novel COntinuous patient state PERceiver model, called COPER, to cope with ITS in EHRs. COPER uses Perceiver model and the concept of neural ordinary differential equations (ODEs) to learn the continuous time dynamics of patient state, i.e., continuity of input space and continuity of output space. The neural ODEs help COPER to generate regular time-series to feed to Perceiver model which has the capability to handle multi-modality large-scale inputs. To evaluate the performance of the proposed model, we use in-hospital mortality prediction task on MIMIC-III dataset and carefully design experiments to study irregularity. The results are compared with the baselines which prove the efficacy of the proposed model.
summary: In electronic health records (EHRs), irregular time-series (ITS) occur naturally due to patient health dynamics, reflected by irregular hospital visits, diseases/conditions and the necessity to measure different vitals signs at each visit etc. ITS present challenges in training machine learning algorithms which mostly are built on assumption of coherent fixed dimensional feature space. In this paper, we propose a novel COntinuous patient state PERceiver model, called COPER, to cope with ITS in EHRs. COPER uses Perceiver model and the concept of neural ordinary differential equations (ODEs) to learn the continuous time dynamics of patient state, i.e., continuity of input space and continuity of output space. The neural ODEs help COPER to generate regular time-series to feed to Perceiver model which has the capability to handle multi-modality large-scale inputs. To evaluate the performance of the proposed model, we use in-hospital mortality prediction task on MIMIC-III dataset and carefully design experiments to study irregularity. The results are compared with the baselines which prove the efficacy of the proposed model.
abstract_zh: 在电子健康记录 (EHR) 中，由于患者健康动态，不规则时间序列 (ITS) 自然发生，表现为不规则的医院就诊、疾病/状况以及每次就诊测量不同生命体征的必要性等。ITS 给训练机器学习算法带来了挑战，这些算法大多建立在一致的固定维度特征空间的假设之上。在本文中，我们提出了一种新颖的连续患者状态 PERceiver 模型，称为 COPER，以应对 EHR 中的 ITS。 COPER使用Perceiver模型和神经常微分方程（ODE）的概念来学习患者状态的连续时间动态，即输入空间的连续性和输出空间的连续性。神经常微分方程帮助 COPER 生成规则的时间序列，以馈送到 Perceiver 模型，该模型能够处理多模态大规模输入。为了评估所提出模型的性能，我们在 MIMIC-III 数据集上使用院内死亡率预测任务，并仔细设计实验来研究不规则性。结果与基线进行比较，证明了所提出模型的有效性。

AI.tldr: 本文提出COPER模型，用于处理电子健康记录中的不规则时间序列问题，通过结合Perceiver模型和神经微分方程实现患者状态的连续学习。
AI.motivation: 传统机器学习方法依赖固定维度特征空间，难以应对电子健康记录中因患者就诊不规律、体征测量差异等导致的不规则时间序列挑战。
AI.method: COPER模型融合Perceiver架构与神经微分方程（ODEs），通过学习患者状态的连续时间动态，将不规则时间序列转化为规则序列，从而处理多模态大规模输入。
AI.result: 在MIMIC-III数据集的住院死亡率预测任务中，COPER的实验结果优于现有基线模型，验证了其在处理不规则时间序列上的有效性。
AI.conclusion: COPER为电子健康记录中的不规则时间序列建模提供了新方法，通过连续状态学习显著提升了临床预测性能。

authors_text: Kurt Shuster,Jing Xu,Mojtaba Komeili,Da Ju,Eric Michael Smith,Stephen Roller,Megan Ung,Moya Chen,Kushal Arora,Joshua Lane,Morteza Behrooz,William Ngan,Spencer Poff,Naman Goyal,Arthur Szlam,Y-Lan Boureau,Melanie Kambadur,Jason Weston
title: BlenderBot 3: a deployed conversational agent that continually learns to responsibly engage
title_zh: BlenderBot 3：一个部署的对话代理，不断学习负责任地参与
abstract: We present BlenderBot 3, a 175B parameter dialogue model capable of open-domain conversation with access to the internet and a long-term memory, and having been trained on a large number of user defined tasks. We release both the model weights and code, and have also deployed the model on a public web page to interact with organic users. This technical report describes how the model was built (architecture, model and training scheme), and details of its deployment, including safety mechanisms. Human evaluations show its superiority to existing open-domain dialogue agents, including its predecessors (Roller et al., 2021; Komeili et al., 2022). Finally, we detail our plan for continual learning using the data collected from deployment, which will also be publicly released. The goal of this research program is thus to enable the community to study ever-improving responsible agents that learn through interaction.
summary: We present BlenderBot 3, a 175B parameter dialogue model capable of open-domain conversation with access to the internet and a long-term memory, and having been trained on a large number of user defined tasks. We release both the model weights and code, and have also deployed the model on a public web page to interact with organic users. This technical report describes how the model was built (architecture, model and training scheme), and details of its deployment, including safety mechanisms. Human evaluations show its superiority to existing open-domain dialogue agents, including its predecessors (Roller et al., 2021; Komeili et al., 2022). Finally, we detail our plan for continual learning using the data collected from deployment, which will also be publicly released. The goal of this research program is thus to enable the community to study ever-improving responsible agents that learn through interaction.
abstract_zh: 我们推出 BlenderBot 3，这是一个 175B 参数对话模型，能够进行开放域对话，可以访问互联网和长期记忆，并且已经接受了大量用户定义任务的训练。我们发布了模型权重和代码，并将模型部署在公共网页上以与有机用户进行交互。该技术报告描述了模型的构建方式（架构、模型和训练方案）及其部署细节，包括安全机制。人类评估表明它优于现有的开放域对话代理，包括其前身（Roller 等人，2021；Komeili 等人，2022）。最后，我们详细介绍了使用从部署中收集的数据进行持续学习的计划，该计划也将公开发布。因此，该研究计划的目标是使社区能够研究通过交互学习的不断改进的负责任的代理。

AI.tldr: 本文介绍了BlenderBot 3，一个具有175B参数的对话模型，能够通过互联网访问和长期记忆进行开放领域对话，并部署在公共网页上与用户交互。
AI.motivation: 旨在开发一个能够持续学习并负责任地与用户互动的对话代理，通过公开部署和数据收集推动社区研究更安全、更智能的对话系统。
AI.method: 构建了包含特定架构和训练方案的模型，结合互联网访问能力和长期记忆机制，并部署了安全机制。通过用户交互数据进行持续学习，计划公开模型权重、代码及收集的数据。
AI.result: 人类评估显示其性能优于现有开放领域对话代理（包括其前身模型），且具备更安全的交互能力。
AI.conclusion: 该研究计划通过持续学习和公开资源，推动社区开发更负责任且不断优化的对话代理，促进交互式AI的长期发展。

authors_text: Aditeya Pandey,Arjun Srinivasan,Vidya Setlur
title: MEDLEY: Intent-based Recommendations to Support Dashboard Composition
title_zh: MEDLEY：支持仪表板组合的基于意图的建议
comment: 11 pages, 8 figures, 2 tables, accepted to IEEE Transaction on Visualization and Graphics (also appearing in the proceedings of IEEE VIS 2022)
comments: 11 pages, 8 figures, 2 tables, accepted to IEEE Transaction on Visualization and Graphics (also appearing in the proceedings of IEEE VIS 2022)
abstract: Despite the ever-growing popularity of dashboards across a wide range of domains, their authoring still remains a tedious and complex process. Current tools offer considerable support for creating individual visualizations but provide limited support for discovering groups of visualizations that can be collectively useful for composing analytic dashboards. To address this problem, we present MEDLEY, a mixed-initiative interface that assists in dashboard composition by recommending dashboard collections (i.e., a logically grouped set of views and filtering widgets) that map to specific analytical intents. Users can specify dashboard intents (namely, measure analysis, change analysis, category analysis, or distribution analysis) explicitly through an input panel in the interface or implicitly by selecting data attributes and views of interest. The system recommends collections based on these analytic intents, and views and widgets can be selected to compose a variety of dashboards. MEDLEY also provides a lightweight direct manipulation interface to configure interactions between views in a dashboard. Based on a study with 13 participants performing both targeted and open-ended tasks, we discuss how MEDLEY's recommendations guide dashboard composition and facilitate different user workflows. Observations from the study identify potential directions for future work, including combining manual view specification with dashboard recommendations and designing natural language interfaces for dashboard authoring.
summary: Despite the ever-growing popularity of dashboards across a wide range of domains, their authoring still remains a tedious and complex process. Current tools offer considerable support for creating individual visualizations but provide limited support for discovering groups of visualizations that can be collectively useful for composing analytic dashboards. To address this problem, we present MEDLEY, a mixed-initiative interface that assists in dashboard composition by recommending dashboard collections (i.e., a logically grouped set of views and filtering widgets) that map to specific analytical intents. Users can specify dashboard intents (namely, measure analysis, change analysis, category analysis, or distribution analysis) explicitly through an input panel in the interface or implicitly by selecting data attributes and views of interest. The system recommends collections based on these analytic intents, and views and widgets can be selected to compose a variety of dashboards. MEDLEY also provides a lightweight direct manipulation interface to configure interactions between views in a dashboard. Based on a study with 13 participants performing both targeted and open-ended tasks, we discuss how MEDLEY's recommendations guide dashboard composition and facilitate different user workflows. Observations from the study identify potential directions for future work, including combining manual view specification with dashboard recommendations and designing natural language interfaces for dashboard authoring.
abstract_zh: 尽管仪表板在各个领域越来越受欢迎，但其创作仍然是一个繁琐而复杂的过程。当前的工具为创建单独的可视化提供了大量支持，但为发现可共同用于构建分析仪表板的可视化组提供有限的支持。为了解决这个问题，我们提出了 MEDLEY，这是一个混合主动界面，它通过推荐映射到特定分析意图的仪表板集合（即逻辑分组的视图和过滤小部件）来帮助仪表板组合。用户可以通过界面中的输入面板显式指定仪表板意图（即度量分析、变化分析、类别分析或分布分析），也可以通过选择感兴趣的数据属性和视图隐式指定仪表板意图。系统根据这些分析意图推荐集合，并且可以选择视图和小部件来组成各种仪表板。 MEDLEY 还提供了一个轻量级的直接操作界面来配置仪表板中视图之间的交互。基于 13 名参与者执行有针对性和开放式任务的研究，我们讨论 MEDLEY 的建议如何指导仪表板组成并促进不同的用户工作流程。该研究的观察结果确定了未来工作的潜在方向，包括将手动视图规范与仪表板建议相结合，以及为仪表板创作设计自然语言界面。

AI.tldr: 本文提出MEDLEY系统，通过基于分析意图的推荐来辅助用户高效创建分析仪表盘。
AI.motivation: 现有工具难以发现可组合成有用仪表盘的可视化组，导致仪表盘创作过程繁琐且复杂。
AI.method: 开发混合主动接口MEDLEY，根据用户显式或隐式指定的分析意图（如趋势分析、分类分析等）推荐可视化集合，并提供直接交互配置界面。
AI.result: 13名参与者的实验表明，MEDLEY的推荐能有效指导仪表盘组合，支持多样化工作流程，同时提出未来需结合手动指定与自然语言接口的改进方向。
AI.conclusion: MEDLEY通过意图驱动的推荐和交互配置优化了仪表盘创作，未来需进一步融合手动方法与自然语言交互以提升体验。

authors_text: Xue Hu,Xinghui Li,Benjamin Busam,Yiren Zhou,Ales Leonardis,Shanxin Yuan
title: Disentangling 3D Attributes from a Single 2D Image: Human Pose, Shape and Garment
title_zh: 从单个 2D 图像中分离出 3D 属性：人体姿势、形状和服装
abstract: For visual manipulation tasks, we aim to represent image content with semantically meaningful features. However, learning implicit representations from images often lacks interpretability, especially when attributes are intertwined. We focus on the challenging task of extracting disentangled 3D attributes only from 2D image data. Specifically, we focus on human appearance and learn implicit pose, shape and garment representations of dressed humans from RGB images. Our method learns an embedding with disentangled latent representations of these three image properties and enables meaningful re-assembling of features and property control through a 2D-to-3D encoder-decoder structure. The 3D model is inferred solely from the feature map in the learned embedding space. To the best of our knowledge, our method is the first to achieve cross-domain disentanglement for this highly under-constrained problem. We qualitatively and quantitatively demonstrate our framework's ability to transfer pose, shape, and garments in 3D reconstruction on virtual data and show how an implicit shape loss can benefit the model's ability to recover fine-grained reconstruction details.
summary: For visual manipulation tasks, we aim to represent image content with semantically meaningful features. However, learning implicit representations from images often lacks interpretability, especially when attributes are intertwined. We focus on the challenging task of extracting disentangled 3D attributes only from 2D image data. Specifically, we focus on human appearance and learn implicit pose, shape and garment representations of dressed humans from RGB images. Our method learns an embedding with disentangled latent representations of these three image properties and enables meaningful re-assembling of features and property control through a 2D-to-3D encoder-decoder structure. The 3D model is inferred solely from the feature map in the learned embedding space. To the best of our knowledge, our method is the first to achieve cross-domain disentanglement for this highly under-constrained problem. We qualitatively and quantitatively demonstrate our framework's ability to transfer pose, shape, and garments in 3D reconstruction on virtual data and show how an implicit shape loss can benefit the model's ability to recover fine-grained reconstruction details.
abstract_zh: 对于视觉操作任务，我们的目标是用语义上有意义的特征来表示图像内容。然而，从图像中学习隐式表示通常缺乏可解释性，尤其是当属性交织在一起时。我们专注于仅从 2D 图像数据中提取解缠结的 3D 属性这一具有挑战性的任务。具体来说，我们关注人类外观，并从 RGB 图像中学习穿着人类的隐含姿势、形状和服装表示。我们的方法通过这三个图像属性的解开潜在表示来学习嵌入，并通过 2D 到 3D 编码器-解码器结构实现特征和属性控制的有意义的重新组装。 3D 模型仅根据学习的嵌入空间中的特征图推断出来。据我们所知，我们的方法是第一个针对这个高度约束问题实现跨域解缠的方法。我们定性和定量地展示了我们的框架在虚拟数据的 3D 重建中传输姿势、形状和服装的能力，并展示了隐式形状损失如何有利于模型恢复细粒度重建细节的能力。

AI.tldr: 本文旨在从单张2D图像中分离出人体的3D属性，包括姿势、形状和服装。
AI.motivation: 现有方法在从图像学习隐式表示时缺乏可解释性，且属性间相互交织，难以独立控制。
AI.method: 通过2D到3D的编码器-解码器结构，学习解耦的潜在表示，并在嵌入空间中利用特征图推断3D模型。
AI.result: 在虚拟数据上验证了框架在姿态、形状和服装迁移中的有效性，并展示了隐式形状损失对细节重建的提升。
AI.conclusion: 本文首次实现该高度欠约束问题的跨域解耦，为视觉操作任务提供了新的方法支持。

authors_text: Dragi Kocev,Nikola Simidjievski,Ana Kostovska,Ivica Dimitrovski,Žiga Kokalj
title: Discover the Mysteries of the Maya: Selected Contributions from the Machine Learning Challenge & The Discovery Challenge Workshop at ECML PKDD 2021
title_zh: 探索玛雅人的奥秘：ECML PKDD 2021 机器学习挑战赛和发现挑战研讨会的精选贡献
comment: Chapter authors. Chapter 1: Matthew Painter and Iris Kramer; Chapter 2: Jürgen Landauer, Burkhard Hoppenstedt, and Johannes Allgaier; Chapter 3: Thorben Hellweg, Stefan Oehmcke, Ankit Kariryaa, Fabian Gieseke, and Christian Igel; Chapter 4: Christian Ayala, Carlos Aranda, and Mikel Galar
comments: Chapter authors. Chapter 1: Matthew Painter and Iris Kramer; Chapter 2: Jürgen Landauer, Burkhard Hoppenstedt, and Johannes Allgaier; Chapter 3: Thorben Hellweg, Stefan Oehmcke, Ankit Kariryaa, Fabian Gieseke, and Christian Igel; Chapter 4: Christian Ayala, Carlos Aranda, and Mikel Galar
abstract: The volume contains selected contributions from the Machine Learning Challenge "Discover the Mysteries of the Maya", presented at the Discovery Challenge Track of The European Conference on Machine Learning and Principles and Practice of Knowledge Discovery in Databases (ECML PKDD 2021). Remote sensing has greatly accelerated traditional archaeological landscape surveys in the forested regions of the ancient Maya. Typical exploration and discovery attempts, beside focusing on whole ancient cities, focus also on individual buildings and structures. Recently, there have been several successful attempts of utilizing machine learning for identifying ancient Maya settlements. These attempts, while relevant, focus on narrow areas and rely on high-quality aerial laser scanning (ALS) data which covers only a fraction of the region where ancient Maya were once settled. Satellite image data, on the other hand, produced by the European Space Agency's (ESA) Sentinel missions, is abundant and, more importantly, publicly available. The "Discover the Mysteries of the Maya" challenge aimed at locating and identifying ancient Maya architectures (buildings, aguadas, and platforms) by performing integrated image segmentation of different types of satellite imagery (from Sentinel-1 and Sentinel-2) data and ALS (lidar) data.
summary: The volume contains selected contributions from the Machine Learning Challenge "Discover the Mysteries of the Maya", presented at the Discovery Challenge Track of The European Conference on Machine Learning and Principles and Practice of Knowledge Discovery in Databases (ECML PKDD 2021). Remote sensing has greatly accelerated traditional archaeological landscape surveys in the forested regions of the ancient Maya. Typical exploration and discovery attempts, beside focusing on whole ancient cities, focus also on individual buildings and structures. Recently, there have been several successful attempts of utilizing machine learning for identifying ancient Maya settlements. These attempts, while relevant, focus on narrow areas and rely on high-quality aerial laser scanning (ALS) data which covers only a fraction of the region where ancient Maya were once settled. Satellite image data, on the other hand, produced by the European Space Agency's (ESA) Sentinel missions, is abundant and, more importantly, publicly available. The "Discover the Mysteries of the Maya" challenge aimed at locating and identifying ancient Maya architectures (buildings, aguadas, and platforms) by performing integrated image segmentation of different types of satellite imagery (from Sentinel-1 and Sentinel-2) data and ALS (lidar) data.
abstract_zh: 该卷包含来自机器学习挑战“发现玛雅之谜”的精选贡献，该挑战在欧洲机器学习和数据库知识发现原理与实践会议（ECML PKDD 2021）的发现挑战赛道上提出。遥感极大地加速了古玛雅森林地区的传统考古景观调查。典型的探索和发现尝试，除了关注整个古城外，还关注单个建筑物和结构。最近，利用机器学习来识别古代玛雅定居点的尝试已经取得了几次成功。这些尝试虽然相关，但重点关注狭窄区域，并依赖于高质量航空激光扫描 (ALS) 数据，这些数据仅覆盖古代玛雅人曾经定居的一小部分区域。另一方面，欧洲航天局 (ESA) 哨兵任务产生的卫星图像数据丰富，更重要的是，可以公开获取。 “探索玛雅之谜”挑战赛旨在通过对不同类型的卫星图像（来自 Sentinel-1 和 Sentinel-2）数据和 ALS（激光雷达）数据进行集成图像分割来定位和识别古代玛雅建筑（建筑物、阿瓜达和平台）。

AI.tldr: 本文集收录了ECML PKDD 2021会议上'发现玛雅文明之谜'机器学习挑战赛的成果，旨在通过整合卫星影像与激光雷达数据识别古代玛雅建筑。
AI.motivation: 传统考古依赖局部高精度激光扫描数据，覆盖范围有限；而ESA Sentinel卫星数据公开且覆盖广泛，可填补区域研究空白。
AI.method: 采用多源数据融合方法，结合Sentinel-1/2卫星影像与ALS激光雷达数据，通过图像分割技术识别玛雅建筑、水池及平台等结构。
AI.result: 成功开发出基于卫星与激光雷达数据集成分析的模型，实现了对玛雅遗址的自动化识别与定位。
AI.conclusion: 多源遥感数据融合显著提升古代遗址探测效率，为大范围考古研究提供新范式，未来可拓展至其他文明遗址探测。

ai_content.tldr: 本文集收录了ECML PKDD 2021会议上'发现玛雅文明之谜'机器学习挑战赛的成果，旨在通过整合卫星影像与激光雷达数据识别古代玛雅建筑。
ai_content.motivation: 传统考古依赖局部高精度激光扫描数据，覆盖范围有限；而ESA Sentinel卫星数据公开且覆盖广泛，可填补区域研究空白。
ai_content.method: 采用多源数据融合方法，结合Sentinel-1/2卫星影像与ALS激光雷达数据，通过图像分割技术识别玛雅建筑、水池及平台等结构。
ai_content.result: 成功开发出基于卫星与激光雷达数据集成分析的模型，实现了对玛雅遗址的自动化识别与定位。
ai_content.conclusion: 多源遥感数据融合显著提升古代遗址探测效率，为大范围考古研究提供新范式，未来可拓展至其他文明遗址探测。
